section_label: PediAid v2 Neonatology Formulary — 199 drugs (Neofax-derived, cross-checked)
review_disclaimer: DRAFT — pending clinician review. Verify every dose against your local protocol and current vial concentration before administration. PediAid assumes no liability.

drugs.monitoring: Electrolytes (Na, K, Cl, HCO₃), urine output.
drugs.adverse_effects: Metabolic acidosis, hypokalaemia, hyponatraemia, sulfa hypersensitivity, blood dyscrasias.
drugs.contraindications: Sulfonamide hypersensitivity, hyperchloraemic acidosis, hyponatraemia, hypokalaemia, severe renal/hepatic impairment.

review.notes: Acetazolamide + furosemide combo for PHVD is now AVOIDED — Kennedy 2001 RCT showed worse outcomes.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Then 50 mg/kg over 4 h, then 100 mg/kg over 16 h
doses.comments: Total 300 mg/kg over 21 h. Dilute in D5W (100 mL for ≤ 5 kg, scale up). Anaphylactoid reactions risk during the load.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 1–2 mL of 10% solution (or 0.5–1 mL of 20%) every 6 hours
doses.comments: Bronchospasm risk — pretreat with bronchodilator.

drugs.monitoring: LFTs, INR, paracetamol level (timed from ingestion), creatinine.
drugs.adverse_effects: Anaphylactoid reactions (flushing, urticaria, bronchospasm, hypotension) in 10–20% during IV load — slow infusion, antihistamines.

india_formulations.notes: Oral used for chronic suppression; primary NICU therapy is IV.

doses.duration: 21 days for CNS/disseminated; 14 days for SEM (skin-eye-mouth)
doses.comments: Continue IV until repeat CSF HSV PCR negative for CNS disease.

doses.indication: HSV chronic suppression after IV course

drugs.monitoring: Renal function (Cr, urine output) at baseline + weekly; CBC; LFTs. Adequate hydration during infusion to prevent crystal nephropathy.
drugs.adverse_effects: Crystal nephropathy (avoid by hydration + slow infusion), neutropenia, transaminitis, phlebitis, lethargy/tremor/seizure (high doses or accumulation in renal impairment).
drugs.renal_adjustment: Reduce frequency in renal impairment; monitor creatinine. KCNT for renal failure: see NEJM CASG 2011.
drugs.incompatibilities: Not with other meds in same line — phlebitis risk; pH ~11.

cross_checks.agreement: agrees on 20 mg/kg q8h CNS HSV term

doses.route: IV (rapid push, central or large peripheral vein, follow with NS flush)
doses.frequency: Single push; assess for conversion within 1–2 min
doses.comments: Push as fast as possible — half-life ~10 sec.

doses.indication: PSVT — repeat doses if first does not convert
doses.max_per_dose: 0.3 mg/kg/dose or 12 mg/dose, whichever is lower

drugs.monitoring: Continuous ECG + BP; have crash cart and pacing available.
drugs.adverse_effects: Brief asystole, flushing, dyspnoea, chest tightness, bronchospasm. Effects last seconds.
drugs.contraindications: 2nd/3rd-degree AV block or sick sinus syndrome (without pacemaker); known hypersensitivity.
drugs.incompatibilities: Theophylline / caffeine block adenosine receptors — may reduce efficacy.

cross_checks.agreement: agrees on 0.1 mg/kg → 0.2 mg/kg, max 6/12 mg

review.notes: Most NICU brochures cite max 6 mg/12 mg (PALS); Neofax cites 0.3 mg/kg or 12 mg per dose. Use the lower of the two.

india_formulations.notes: Use 1 mg/mL stock to PREPARE continuous infusions. Dilute before bolus IV use — bolus IV directly from 1 mg/mL is dangerous.

india_formulations.form: IV / IO emergency (1:10,000) — typically prepared from 1:1,000
india_formulations.strength: 0.1 mg/mL — prepare by diluting 1 mL of 1 mg/mL with 9 mL of NS
brands_india: No common pre-filled commercial 1:10,000 in India — usually prepared bedside
india_formulations.notes: ALWAYS use the 0.1 mg/mL concentration for IV/IO bolus during code. NEVER use 1 mg/mL for IV bolus (10× error risk).

doses.indication: Cardiac arrest / Severe bradycardia — IV / IO
doses.infusion_rate: Rapid push, follow with 0.5–1 mL NS flush
doses.comments: Standard NRP/PALS dose. 0.1 mL/kg–0.3 mL/kg of 1:10,000 (0.1 mg/mL).

doses.indication: Cardiac arrest / Severe bradycardia — ETT (when IV access unavailable)
doses.comments: Follow with several positive-pressure ventilations. NEVER give this higher ETT dose IV. IV route is preferred — ETT efficacy not formally proven in neonates.

doses.frequency: Repeat in 5–10 min if needed; may follow with infusion
doses.comments: 'Push-dose pressor' / 'low-dose adrenaline' for the at-risk myocardium. Use 0.01 mg/mL (10 mcg/mL) prep — draw 1 mL of 0.1 mg/mL into 9 mL NS.

doses.indication: Continuous infusion — septic shock (fluid-refractory, dopamine-resistant) / hypotension
doses.dose_per_kg_per_min_range: 0.05–0.3 mcg/kg/min (severe shock); titrate up to 1 mcg/kg/min
doses.comments: Recommended infusion concentration in neonates: 10 mcg/mL. Maximum concentration 60 mcg/mL. Prepare from 1 mg/mL stock (NOT from the 1:10,000 prep).

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 0.1 mL/kg of 1:10,000 (0.1 mg/mL) instilled or rapidly nebulised
doses.comments: Alternative: 0.5 mL of 1:10,000 + 1 mL air in a 5 mL syringe via OG tube to create a splash.

drugs.infusion_preparation: STANDARD NICU 10 mcg/mL PREP: take 0.5 mg of adrenaline (0.5 mL of 1 mg/mL) in 50 mL of D5W → 10 mcg/mL. At 1 mL/kg/hr this = 10 mcg/kg/hr = 0.167 mcg/kg/min. To compute rate for desired dose: rate (mL/hr) = dose (mcg/kg/min) × wt (kg) × 6 / [conc (mcg/mL)].
drugs.monitoring: Continuous HR + BP, IV site for infiltration (extravasation → tissue ischaemia and necrosis — treat with phentolamine 1 mg/mL local infiltration, 1–5 mL).
drugs.adverse_effects: Tachyarrhythmia (PVCs, VT), hypertension, hyperglycaemia, lactic acidosis, renal vascular ischaemia at higher doses. Bolus dose hypertension may cause IVH in preterm.
drugs.contraindications: None absolute in cardiac arrest. Caution with pre-existing tachyarrhythmia.
drugs.reconstitution: 1 mg/mL ready ampoule. For 0.1 mg/mL emergency bolus prep: 1 mL of 1 mg/mL + 9 mL of NS in a 10 mL syringe. Discard 30 min after dilution.
drugs.incompatibilities: Do NOT mix with: aminophylline, ampicillin, hyaluronidase, micafungin, sodium bicarbonate. NS infusion alone is acceptable but D5W is preferred (dextrose protects against oxidation).

cross_checks.source: WHO Model Formulary for Children 2024 — epinephrine
cross_checks.agreement: agrees on 0.01 mg/kg IV in arrest, infusion 0.1–1 mcg/kg/min
agrees on 0.01–0.03 mg/kg IV/IO; 0.05–0.1 mg/kg ETT
agrees on 0.01 mg/kg IV/IO q3–5 min in arrest

cross_checks.source: DailyMed Adrenalin 1 mg/mL injection (Par Pharm) label

review.notes: Critical drug — verify both formulations (1:1,000 vs 1:10,000) on the label before every dose. mg/mL ↔ mcg/mL ↔ ratio confusion is the leading source of paediatric drug error.

doses.max_per_day_all_routes: 3 g/kg (= 60 mL/kg) in the first hour or until perfusion improves / hepatomegaly develops

doses.frequency: Single dose ~ 1 hr before exchange transfusion

drugs.monitoring: BP, HR, SpO₂, perfusion; serum albumin; signs of fluid overload.
drugs.adverse_effects: Volume overload, mild allergic reactions (rare).
drugs.contraindications: Severe anaemia / heart failure (without prior transfusion).

review.notes: Crystalloid (NS) is preferred first-line over albumin for routine volume resuscitation. Consider albumin in shock with low oncotic pressure.

india_formulations.notes: Avoid 20-mL multidose bottles in neonates — benzalkonium chloride may worsen bronchospasm.

doses.comments: Use other K-lowering measures if K > 7.5 mmol/L.

drugs.monitoring: HR (tachycardia), tremor, K (transient drop), glucose (transient rise).
drugs.adverse_effects: Tachycardia, tremor, hypokalaemia, hyperglycaemia, agitation.

doses.indication: Maintain ductal patency in ductal-dependent congenital heart disease
doses.route: IV continuous infusion (UAC tip near ductus also acceptable)
doses.dose_per_kg_per_min_range: 0.01–0.1 mcg/kg/min (titrate to oxygenation vs adverse effects)
doses.comments: Maintenance can be as low as 0.01 mcg/kg/min. Higher initial doses are NOT more effective and have higher AE rates.

drugs.monitoring: Continuous SpO₂ + HR + BP + RR; apnoea monitor; temperature.
drugs.adverse_effects: Apnoea (10–12% of treated neonates, especially < 2 kg, usually within first hour) — be ready for intubation. Fever, hypotension, flushing, jitteriness, diarrhoea. Long-term: cortical hyperostosis, antral hyperplasia.
drugs.contraindications: Respiratory distress syndrome where PGE1 worsens hypoxaemia (rare); known hypersensitivity.
drugs.black_box_warning: Apnoea — risk in 10–12% of treated neonates with CHD, especially < 2 kg birth weight, usually within first hour of infusion. Have ventilation support immediately available.

review.notes: Always intubate / prepare to intubate before transport on PGE1.

doses.indication: Restoration of central venous catheter patency (occlusion)
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 1 mg/mL — instil 110% of internal lumen volume (max 2 mg in 2 mL); a lower 0.5 mg dose has been used in infants ≤ 10 kg

doses.dose_per_kg_per_min_range: Up to 0.5 mg/kg/hour catheter-directed; reduce to 0.1 mg/kg/hr if local bleeding (stop 1 h, restart lower)
doses.comments: Discontinue heparin several hours before starting. PT, aPTT, fibrinogen, platelets at baseline + every 4–6 h.

drugs.monitoring: PT, aPTT, fibrinogen (keep > 1 g/L), platelets (keep > 50k), bleeding signs, cranial USG (preterm).
drugs.adverse_effects: Major bleeding (esp. intracranial, GI), hypotension, anaphylaxis (rare).
drugs.contraindications: Active major bleeding, recent surgery / trauma / IVH, severe HTN, GA < 32 weeks (relative — IVH risk), known hypersensitivity.

review.notes: Alteplase use in extreme preterm requires urgent multidisciplinary review (neonatology, paediatric haematology, IR).

doses.comments: Source: Smits / Rivera-Chaparro 2017. Target peak > 24 mg/L, trough < 3 mg/L.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 14 mg/kg q48h / 12 mg/kg q36h / continued q36h

drugs.monitoring: Therapeutic levels — Peak (1 h post infusion) > 24 mg/L (target 25–35); Trough (just pre-next-dose) < 3 mg/L. Audiology screen at discharge for any > 5-day exposure.
drugs.adverse_effects: Nephrotoxicity, ototoxicity (often irreversible — vestibular and auditory), neuromuscular blockade in concurrent use of Mg/curariform agents.
drugs.contraindications: Aminoglycoside hypersensitivity; severe renal impairment without level monitoring.
drugs.black_box_warning: Aminoglycoside therapy: neurotoxicity, ototoxicity, nephrotoxicity. Risk increased with renal impairment, dehydration, prolonged therapy. Discontinue or adjust if ototoxicity or nephrotoxicity appears. Often irreversible.
drugs.incompatibilities: Do NOT co-infuse with β-lactams (ampicillin / penicillin G inactivate aminoglycosides in vitro).

cross_checks.agreement: primary source for the weight × age table

review.notes: Some Indian units use 15 mg/kg once daily for term neonates; verify local protocol.

drugs.monitoring: PT/aPTT, fibrinogen, D-dimer, evidence of thrombus on ECMO circuit, RFT.
drugs.adverse_effects: Thrombosis (especially on stagnant catheters), hypotension, hyperkalaemia.
drugs.contraindications: DIC without concurrent anticoagulation, active intravascular thrombus, severe renal impairment.

review.notes: Tranexamic acid is an alternative antifibrinolytic.

doses.frequency: every 8–12 hours (start 8–12 h after the load)
doses.comments: Aminophylline is 80% theophylline by weight. Equivalent oral theophylline dose = aminophylline dose × 0.8.

drugs.monitoring: Therapeutic theophylline 5–12 mcg/mL (apnoea), 10–20 (bronchodilation). HR (tachycardia), feeding intolerance, glucose.
drugs.adverse_effects: Tachycardia, irritability, vomiting, feeding intolerance, hyperglycaemia, seizure (toxic levels), GI bleeding.

review.notes: Caffeine is preferred — wider therapeutic index, less monitoring.

doses.indication: Refractory VT / VF / SVT — IV loading
doses.comments: Correct K, Mg, Ca before initiating. May repeat × 1–2 doses up to total 15 mg/kg.
Concentration ≤ 2 mg/mL via peripheral; > 2 mg/mL only via central line. Switch to oral within 24–48 h.

drugs.monitoring: Continuous ECG (QTc, AV block); BP during loading; LFTs, TFTs at baseline + serially; pulmonary review.
drugs.adverse_effects: Pulmonary toxicity (interstitial pneumonitis), hepatotoxicity, hypothyroidism / hyperthyroidism, blue-grey skin discolouration, corneal microdeposits, photosensitivity. IV: hypotension, bradycardia.
drugs.contraindications: Cardiogenic shock, sick sinus syndrome / 2nd–3rd degree AV block (without pacemaker), severe sinus bradycardia, iodine allergy.
drugs.black_box_warning: Pulmonary, hepatic, and cardiac toxicity. Use only for life-threatening arrhythmias — substantial toxicity. May proarrhythmia (QT prolongation, torsades).

cross_checks.agreement: agrees on 5 mg/kg IV load over 20–60 min

review.notes: Avoid undiluted bolus in pulseless VT/VF — give over 1–2 min for cardiac arrest only.

doses.indication: Mild bacterial infection / completion oral therapy
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Manufacturer max 30 mg/kg/day divided every 12 h

drugs.monitoring: Clinical response.
drugs.adverse_effects: Rash, diarrhoea, candidiasis.

review.notes: Oral amoxicillin is used for outpatient completion of treatment after IV ampicillin.

drugs.monitoring: Daily renal function (Cr), K, Mg, LFTs, CBC.
drugs.adverse_effects: Nephrotoxicity (high), infusion reactions (fever, chills, rigors), hypokalaemia, hypomagnesaemia, anaemia, transaminitis, phlebitis.
drugs.incompatibilities: Do NOT mix with NS (precipitates) — use D5W only.

review.notes: Lipid formulations preferred when affordable due to better tolerability.

doses.indication: Invasive Candida (alternative to fluconazole / conventional ampho)
doses.duration: ≥ 2 weeks after blood culture clearance + symptom resolution

drugs.monitoring: Renal function, K, Mg, LFTs, CBC.
drugs.adverse_effects: Less nephrotoxic than conventional amphotericin; infusion reactions (fever, chills), hypokalaemia, hypomagnesaemia, transaminitis.

india_formulations.strength: 250 mg, 500 mg, 1 g, 2 g vials (some 5 g and 10 g hospital packs)
india_formulations.notes: Reconstitute with sterile water. Use within 1 hour of mixing — loses potency.
Oral form rarely used in NICU; IV preferred for systemic infection.

doses.indication: Sepsis / Bacteraemia (empirical, GBS) — early-onset, with aminoglycoside
doses.duration: 10 days for bacteraemia without focus; longer if complicated
doses.comments: Use with aminoglycoside (gentamicin) for empirical EOS therapy. Source: Puopolo 2019 / AAP.

doses.indication: General dosage — by PMA (alternative interval guide)
doses.frequency: every 12 h (postnatal 0–28 d), then every 8 h
every 12 h (0–14 d), every 8 h (> 14 d)
every 12 h (0–7 d), every 8 h (> 7 d)

drugs.administration: IV — doses ≤ 500 mg over 3–5 min slow push; doses ≥ 1 g over 10–15 min. IV concentrations 30, 40, 50, 100 mg/mL. IM — final concentration 250 mg/mL.
drugs.monitoring: Periodic CBC, LFT, RFT on extended therapy. Bleeding time may prolong by ~60 sec after 3rd–4th dose.
drugs.adverse_effects: Rash, fever (rare in neonates). CNS excitation/seizures with very large doses. Mild prolongation of bleeding time. Hypersensitivity (uncommon in neonates).
drugs.renal_adjustment: PMA-driven dosing already accounts for renal maturation; no further adjustment usually needed in absence of overt AKI.
drugs.incompatibilities: Do NOT mix with: amikacin, amiodarone, dopamine, epinephrine, fluconazole, gentamicin, hydralazine, midazolam, sodium bicarbonate, tobramycin. Compatible with: acyclovir, aztreonam, calcium gluconate, cefepime, heparin, hydrocortisone succinate, insulin, magnesium sulfate, metronidazole, milrinone, morphine, propofol, vancomycin.
drugs.reconstitution: Reconstitute powder vial with sterile water for injection. Maximum IV concentration 100 mg/mL. IM concentration 250 mg/mL. Use reconstituted solution within 1 hour.

cross_checks.source: WHO Model Formulary for Children 2024 — ampicillin
cross_checks.agreement: agrees on 50 mg/kg q12h (week 1), q8h (weeks 2–4); meningitis 100 mg/kg q8h
agrees — primary source for these tables

doses.indication: Invasive candidiasis (term, intolerant of fluconazole / amphotericin)
doses.comments: Higher doses may be needed on ECMO.

drugs.monitoring: LFTs, CBC.
drugs.adverse_effects: Transaminitis, infusion-related histamine reactions (rare).

review.notes: Caspofungin is more frequently used in NICU than anidulafungin.

drugs.category: Urea cycle drug — nitrogen pathway substrate

doses.indication: Acute hyperammonaemia (urea cycle defect, pending diagnosis)
doses.dose_per_kg_per_min_starting: 600 mg/kg/day continuous infusion (or 250 mg/kg/day with bolus regimen)
doses.comments: Always co-administer with sodium phenylacetate + sodium benzoate (Ammonul) and dextrose for caloric load.

drugs.monitoring: Plasma ammonia (every 2–4 h), electrolytes, glucose, ABG, plasma amino acids, BP.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hyperchloraemic acidosis, hyperkalaemia, hyperphosphataemia, vasodilation / hypotension on rapid load.

review.notes: Initiate only with metabolic medicine team.

drugs.monitoring: Routine clinical.
drugs.adverse_effects: GI upset (high oral doses), oxalate stones (chronic mega-doses).
drugs.contraindications: G6PD deficiency (relative — high IV doses cause haemolysis).

drugs.monitoring: Bleeding, GI symptoms, platelet count, signs of Reye syndrome (avoid in viral illness — varicella / influenza).
drugs.adverse_effects: GI bleed, Reye syndrome (during viral illness), bronchospasm (NSAID-sensitive asthma).
drugs.contraindications: Active bleeding, varicella / influenza (Reye risk), severe hepatic / renal impairment.

india_formulations.notes: Oral atropine rarely used in NICU.

doses.route: IV (over 1 min, immediately before other premeds)

doses.loading_dose_per_kg: 0.02 mg/kg, doubled every 5 min until atropinisation
doses.dose_per_kg_per_min_range: Maintenance infusion 10–20% of total loading dose / hr (max 2 mg/hr)

drugs.monitoring: HR, BP, pupils, mucous membranes (dry), bowel sounds.
drugs.adverse_effects: Tachycardia, mydriasis, dry mucous membranes, urinary retention, fever, paradoxical bradycardia at very low doses (theoretical in neonates — rarely clinically significant).

cross_checks.agreement: agrees on 0.02 mg/kg IV/IO, max 0.5 mg single dose

review.notes: Many guidelines recommend min single dose 0.1 mg to avoid theoretical paradoxical bradycardia; data in true neonates are limited.

drugs.monitoring: Watch for hypertrophic pyloric stenosis (esp. < 6 weeks), QT prolongation if comorbidities.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypertrophic pyloric stenosis (KIDs list — risk in neonates), QT prolongation, cholestatic hepatitis, pseudomembranous colitis.
drugs.contraindications: Macrolide hypersensitivity, history of cholestatic jaundice with macrolides.

review.notes: KIDs list — avoid in neonates UNLESS specific indication (pertussis / chlamydia / ureaplasma).

drugs.monitoring: Clinical response; CBC, RFT.
drugs.adverse_effects: Rash, eosinophilia, transaminitis, neutropenia.
drugs.contraindications: Aztreonam hypersensitivity (true cross-reactivity with penicillins is minimal).

review.notes: Best option for gram-negative coverage in true severe penicillin allergy.

doses.route: Intratracheal (4 quarter-dose aliquots in different positions)
doses.frequency: First dose within 15 min of birth; up to 4 doses in first 48 h, no more frequently than every 6 h
First dose by 8 h of age; up to 4 doses in first 48 h, no more frequently than every 6 h
doses.comments: Confirm RDS on chest X-ray before subsequent doses.

drugs.administration: Warm vial to room temperature (do NOT shake). Suction ETT before. Disconnect from ventilator briefly between aliquots. Reconnect, hand-bag for 15–30 sec. Re-position infant for next aliquot. Do not suction for at least 1 hour after dosing unless airway obstruction.
drugs.monitoring: Continuous SpO₂ + HR + chest expansion during instillation. Watch for transient bradycardia / desaturation, ETT obstruction, pneumothorax.
drugs.adverse_effects: Transient bradycardia, desaturation, reflux up the ETT, pulmonary haemorrhage (rare), air leak (PIE, pneumothorax).

review.notes: Indian NICUs increasingly use poractant alfa (Curosurf) due to evidence of lower mortality at 200 mg/kg. Verify local availability.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 0.625 mg in 0.025 mL per eye (single dose)
doses.comments: Lower doses 0.031–0.25 mg in 0.025 mL also used in dose-de-escalation studies. Retreatment may be needed beyond 55 weeks PMA.

drugs.monitoring: Serial retinal exam, plus systemic monitoring (BP, growth) due to systemic VEGF suppression.
drugs.adverse_effects: Possible systemic VEGF suppression — concern about lung and kidney development. Endophthalmitis (rare procedural).
drugs.contraindications: Active intraocular infection, severe ocular hypertension.

review.notes: Long-term neurodevelopmental safety incomplete — discuss with family. Aflibercept and ranibizumab are alternatives.

doses.frequency: every 24 hours in first 2 months of life, then every 12 hours

doses.ga_band: Preterm ≥ 34 weeks and term, first month
doses.frequency: every 24 hours, then every 12 hours after 1 month

drugs.monitoring: Electrolytes, urine output, weight, hearing if prolonged use.
drugs.adverse_effects: Similar to furosemide but less ototoxicity; hypokalaemia, hyponatraemia, hypochloraemia, hypocalcaemia, hypomagnesaemia.

review.notes: 1 mg bumetanide ≈ 40 mg furosemide.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Use lowest effective dose / concentration. Specific technique-driven doses; consult an anaesthetist.
doses.max_per_dose: 2 mg/kg single dose (continuous infusion typical max 0.2–0.4 mg/kg/hr in neonates)

drugs.monitoring: ECG, BP, neurologic status; methaemoglobin level if cyanosis develops.
drugs.adverse_effects: Cardiotoxicity (worst with rapid IV exposure), seizure, methaemoglobinaemia, respiratory depression with high spinal block.

review.notes: Use only by trained anaesthetist. Not a first-line pain modality on the NICU floor.

drugs.category: Partial opioid agonist (μ partial agonist / κ antagonist)

doses.indication: Neonatal Abstinence Syndrome (NAS) — adjunct or first-line where available
doses.loading_dose_per_kg: Initial 15.9 mcg/kg/day SL in 3 divided doses
doses.comments: Titrate up in 25% increments (max 6 up-titrations). Wean by 10% of stabilisation dose until 10% of initial, then stop.

drugs.monitoring: Finnegan / NAS score, RR, SpO₂, feeding pattern, weight.
drugs.adverse_effects: Sedation, respiratory depression (less than full agonists), constipation.

cross_checks.source: Kraft 2017 NEJM (BBORN trial) — buprenorphine SL vs morphine for NAS

review.notes: Sublingual buprenorphine is shorter NICU stay vs oral morphine in NAS.

india_formulations.strength: 20 mg/mL caffeine citrate (= 10 mg/mL caffeine base) — preservative-free, 3 mL single-use vial
india_formulations.notes: 20 mg caffeine citrate ≡ 10 mg caffeine base. Always document which you mean. Same vial used for IV and oral routes.

doses.route: IV (over 30 min) for load; IV (over 10 min) or PO for maintenance
doses.loading_dose_per_kg: 20 mg/kg of caffeine citrate (= 1 mL/kg of 20 mg/mL solution)
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 5 mg/kg/dose of caffeine citrate (= 0.25 mL/kg of 20 mg/mL)
doses.frequency: Maintenance every 24 hours; start 24 h after the load
doses.comments: Express clearly whether 5 mg/kg refers to citrate or base. This row uses CITRATE.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Maintenance median 8 mg/kg/day (range 5–10 mg/kg/day) of caffeine citrate
doses.duration: Median 60 days (range 46–75 days) until extubation / 33 wk PMA
doses.comments: Higher-dose maintenance (5–10 mg/kg/day citrate) is associated with lower rates of NEC and PDA-ligation in retrospective data.
Start 24 h before extubation.

drugs.monitoring: Routine serum levels usually NOT required. Therapeutic trough 5–25 mcg/mL caffeine; toxic > 40–50 mcg/mL. Periodic glucose. Watch for tachycardia (HR > 180), feeding intolerance, NEC.
drugs.adverse_effects: Tachycardia, agitation, feeding intolerance, hyperglycaemia, diuresis. Higher-dose meta-analysis showed no increase in adverse outcomes vs standard dose.
drugs.renal_adjustment: Monitor levels; renal impairment slows clearance. Adjust as needed.
drugs.hepatic_adjustment: Monitor levels; hepatic impairment slows clearance.
drugs.incompatibilities: See Neofax compatibility list.
drugs.reconstitution: No reconstitution needed — supplied as ready solution. 1 mL = 20 mg caffeine citrate.
drugs.pearl: Caffeine half-life in neonates is 3–4 days (vs ~5 h in adults). Therapeutic effect persists long after stopping the infusion.

cross_checks.source: WHO Model Formulary for Children 2024 — caffeine citrate
cross_checks.agreement: agrees on 20 mg/kg load, 5 mg/kg/day maintenance

doses.indication: Cardiac arrest with documented hypoCa / hyperK / hyperMg / CCB overdose
doses.route: IV slow infusion (over 30–60 min ; CENTRAL line preferred)

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 33 mg of CaCl 10% per 100 mL citrated blood (= 0.33 mL per 100 mL)

drugs.monitoring: ECG (arrhythmia), IV site (severe necrosis on extravasation), serum ionised Ca.
drugs.adverse_effects: Bradycardia, hypotension if rapid; severe tissue necrosis / sloughing if extravasates.
drugs.contraindications: Hypercalcaemia, ventricular fibrillation (relative — only if hypoCa documented), digitalis toxicity.
drugs.incompatibilities: Do NOT mix with bicarbonate or phosphate.

review.notes: 10% CaCl = 100 mg/mL salt = 27 mg/mL elemental Ca = 1.4 mEq/mL elemental Ca. Calcium gluconate generally preferred due to lower acidosis risk.

india_formulations.strength: Various — typically 100 mg/5 mL elemental calcium
india_formulations.notes: 10 % calcium gluconate IV: 100 mg/mL salt = 9.3 mg/mL elemental Ca = 0.465 mEq/mL.

doses.route: IV slow over 10–30 min (NEVER IM/SC — extravasation causes necrosis)
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 10–20 mg/kg elemental Ca (= 100–200 mg/kg/dose calcium gluconate)
doses.frequency: Single dose; follow with continuous infusion if persistent

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 50–80 mg/kg/day elemental Ca (= 538–860 mg/kg/day calcium gluconate)
doses.frequency: Continuous IV for 48 h, then taper; or oral in 3–4 divided doses
Repeat as needed for ECG changes

drugs.administration: Slow infusion only (max 50 mg/min elemental Ca = 500 mg/min gluconate). Confirm IV patency before each dose — extravasation causes severe necrosis.
drugs.monitoring: ECG (look for short QT, ST depression in hypercalcaemia), serum ionised Ca, IV site for infiltration.
drugs.adverse_effects: Bradycardia, hypotension if given too fast. Tissue necrosis on extravasation. Vomiting, flushing, tingling.
drugs.incompatibilities: Do NOT mix with NaHCO₃ or phosphate-containing solutions — precipitate.

cross_checks.agreement: agrees on 100–200 mg/kg/dose for symptomatic hypoCa

review.notes: Always document whether dose is in mg of CALCIUM GLUCONATE (salt) or mg of ELEMENTAL CALCIUM — 10× difference.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 20–80 mg/kg/day elemental Ca, in 2–4 divided doses
Start 20 mg/kg/day elemental Ca + 10–20 mg/kg/day elemental P; titrate to max 70–80 mg/kg/day Ca + 40–50 mg/kg/day P
doses.comments: Always pair Ca with P supplementation in osteopenia.

drugs.monitoring: Serum Ca, P, alk-phos, urine Ca/Cr ratio.
drugs.adverse_effects: Constipation, mild GI upset, milk-alkali syndrome (high dose).

review.notes: Different oral salts have very different elemental Ca per mL — always document mg of elemental Ca, not mL or salt.

doses.frequency: Up to 3 doses every 12 h (or every 6 h in trial protocols if persistently intubated and FiO₂ ≥ 30%)
doses.comments: Prophylaxis: within 30 min of birth; rescue: within 2 h.

drugs.monitoring: Same as beractant — SpO₂, HR, chest expansion, watch for transient bradycardia/desaturation.
drugs.adverse_effects: Transient bradycardia, desaturation, reflux up ETT, rare pulmonary haemorrhage, air leak.

review.notes: Calfactant rarely available in India — poractant alfa (Curosurf) is the local primary surfactant.

doses.comments: Heart failure: 0.4–1.6 mg/kg/day divided every 8 hours. Reduce in renal impairment.

drugs.monitoring: BP, K, Cr (rise > 30% prompts dose review), urine output.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypotension (especially first dose), hyperkalaemia, AKI / oliguria, neutropenia, cough (rare in infants).
drugs.contraindications: Bilateral renal artery stenosis, anuria, prior angioedema.

review.notes: Use with extreme care in preterm — significant Cr drop reported. Always start with a test dose.

drugs.category: Urea cycle drug — N-acetylglutamate synthase analog

doses.indication: Acute hyperammonaemia (NAGS deficiency, propionic acidemia, methylmalonic acidemia)
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 100–250 mg/kg/day in 2–4 divided doses (round to nearest 100 mg)
doses.comments: Initiate as soon as diagnosis suspected. Titrate to maintain plasma ammonia in normal range. Co-administer with other ammonia-lowering therapies (alternate pathway drugs, dialysis, protein restriction).

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Long-term doses individualised by metabolic specialist; typical 10–100 mg/kg/day divided

drugs.monitoring: Plasma ammonia, urea cycle metabolites, weight, growth, neurology.
drugs.adverse_effects: Vomiting, diarrhoea, abdominal pain.

review.notes: Specialty drug — initiate only with metabolic medicine team.

drugs.monitoring: LFTs, CBC, electrolytes.
drugs.adverse_effects: Transaminitis, infusion reactions (rare in neonates), histamine-mediated flushing if rapid.

cross_checks.agreement: agrees on m²-based dosing in neonates

review.notes: Body surface area: BSA(m²) = √[(wt × ht)/3600] ≈ ~0.2–0.25 for term neonate.

doses.indication: Skin / soft tissue / bone-joint / surgical prophylaxis

drugs.monitoring: Clinical response; CBC, RFT for prolonged use.
drugs.adverse_effects: Rash, eosinophilia, transaminitis, rare neutropenia, phlebitis.

drugs.monitoring: Clinical response; CBC, RFT; neuro status (cefepime-induced encephalopathy in renal impairment).
drugs.adverse_effects: Rash, eosinophilia, transaminitis, neutropenia. Cefepime-induced encephalopathy / NCSE in renal impairment — caution in oliguric infants.

doses.comments: Smaller doses / longer intervals for VLBW < 2 kg.

drugs.monitoring: Clinical response; CBC, RFT; LFTs.
drugs.adverse_effects: Rash, eosinophilia, transaminitis, neutropenia, phlebitis.
drugs.incompatibilities: Do not mix with sodium bicarbonate, aminoglycosides in the same line.

review.notes: Often supply-constrained globally — many units now use cefepime instead.

drugs.monitoring: Clinical response; CBC, RFT.
drugs.adverse_effects: Same as other cephalosporins.

drugs.monitoring: Clinical response; CBC, RFT.
drugs.adverse_effects: Rash, eosinophilia, transaminitis, neutropenia.

drugs.monitoring: Bilirubin (caution in jaundice — displaces from albumin); clinical response.
drugs.adverse_effects: Diarrhoea, biliary pseudolithiasis, displacement of bilirubin from albumin → kernicterus risk in neonates, especially preterm.
drugs.contraindications: PRETERM NEONATES — contraindicated. Hyperbilirubinaemia. Concurrent administration with IV calcium-containing solutions (including PN) in any neonate — fatal Ca-ceftriaxone precipitates have been reported.
drugs.black_box_warning: Contraindicated in premature neonates and any neonate with hyperbilirubinaemia. Concurrent use with IV calcium-containing solutions in any neonate is contraindicated — fatal precipitation in lung and kidney.

review.notes: AVOID in any neonate < 28 days if calcium-containing PN is running — switch to cefotaxime or cefepime.

doses.frequency: Single dose; may repeat once after 30 min if inadequate

drugs.monitoring: RR, SpO₂, HR continuously during and after sedation; airway patency.
drugs.adverse_effects: Long unpredictable half-life; respiratory depression, hypotension, GI upset, paradoxical excitation. Reports of sudden death — falling out of favour.
drugs.contraindications: Hepatic / renal impairment, GI ulceration, hypersensitivity.

review.notes: Oral / rectal product no longer manufactured in some countries. Long half-life and sudden death reports — POOR CHOICE for routine procedural sedation in 2024+.

doses.indication: Severe gram-negative infection (with level monitoring only)

drugs.monitoring: Therapeutic peak 15–25 mcg/mL; trough 5–10 mcg/mL. CBC weekly (aplastic anaemia).
drugs.adverse_effects: Grey baby syndrome (cardiovascular collapse, cyanosis, hypothermia, abdominal distension), aplastic anaemia (idiosyncratic), bone marrow suppression (dose-related).
drugs.contraindications: Use without serum monitoring in neonates (KIDs list), prior aplastic anaemia.
drugs.black_box_warning: Serious and fatal blood dyscrasias (aplastic anaemia, hypoplastic anaemia, thrombocytopenia, granulocytopenia) have occurred — use only when other antibiotics are contraindicated or ineffective.

review.notes: Last-resort drug in modern NICU; almost never used in developed centres.

doses.comments: Administer with food (improves absorption). IV not recommended due to lack of data.

drugs.monitoring: Electrolytes (Na, K, Cl), urine output.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypokalaemia, hyponatraemia, hypochloraemic alkalosis, hypercalcaemia, hyperuricaemia.

review.notes: Often used in combination with spironolactone for BPD.

india_formulations.strength: 200 mg, 400 mg tab; 200 mg/5 mL syrup

drugs.monitoring: RFT (renal clearance), drug interactions.
drugs.adverse_effects: Many drug interactions (CYP inhibition), bradycardia (with rapid IV), thrombocytopenia, gynaecomastia, transaminitis.

review.notes: Largely replaced by ranitidine and famotidine due to drug interactions; ranitidine itself withdrawn over NDMA — most NICU use famotidine now.

drugs.monitoring: Stool pattern (C. diff colitis), LFTs on prolonged use.
drugs.adverse_effects: Diarrhoea, C. difficile colitis (black-box class warning), rash, transaminitis, rare blood dyscrasias.
drugs.black_box_warning: Clostridioides difficile-associated diarrhoea (CDAD) reported with virtually all antibacterials, including clindamycin — may range from mild diarrhoea to fatal colitis.

doses.comments: Titrate to NAS scoring; discontinue gradually based on stability.

drugs.monitoring: HR, BP, sedation level.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypotension, bradycardia, sedation. Rebound hypertension on abrupt withdrawal.

review.notes: Adjunct only — primary NAS treatment is morphine or buprenorphine.

doses.comments: PICOLO trial — 0.2 mg/kg/day in infants matches adult 75 mg/day platelet inhibition (~30–50% suppression of ADP-induced aggregation).

drugs.monitoring: Bleeding, platelet count.
drugs.adverse_effects: Bleeding, thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura (rare), GI upset.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 2.5–5 mg/kg/day of colistin BASE in 2–4 divided doses
doses.max_per_day_all_routes: 5 mg/kg/day colistin base activity (normal renal function)
doses.comments: Dosing terminology is confusing — colistin base activity (CBA) ≈ 30 mg of CMS = 1 million IU. Document the unit.

drugs.monitoring: RFT (daily), urine output, electrolytes, neuro status, audiology.
drugs.adverse_effects: Nephrotoxicity (dose-related), neurotoxicity (paraesthesia, ataxia, neuromuscular blockade), bronchospasm (nebulised), eosinophilia.
drugs.contraindications: Severe renal impairment without level monitoring; hypersensitivity.

review.notes: Reserve for confirmed MDR / XDR gram-negatives. Combine with another active agent.

doses.indication: Adrenal insufficiency — diagnostic test (FDA standard dose)
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 0.125 mg per dose (= 0.5 mL of 0.25 mg/mL) ; alternatively 0.015 mg/kg IV
doses.frequency: Single dose; sample cortisol at 0, 30, 60 min

doses.comments: Withhold cortisone, hydrocortisone, spironolactone on test day.

drugs.monitoring: Cortisol response.
drugs.adverse_effects: Rare hypersensitivity.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 1–2 drops in eye 10–30 min before exam
doses.comments: Use only 0.5 % concentration in neonates. Often combined with phenylephrine 2.5 %. Apply pressure to lacrimal sac during + 2 min after to minimise systemic absorption.

drugs.monitoring: HR, BP, GI distension (anticholinergic systemic effects), feeding tolerance.
drugs.adverse_effects: Tachycardia, abdominal distension / ileus, transient hyperglycaemia, behavioural disturbance.

doses.indication: Facilitation of extubation / BPD prevention (DART low-dose)
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Day 1–3: 0.075 mg/kg q12h ; Day 4–6: 0.05 mg/kg q12h ; Day 7–8: 0.025 mg/kg q12h ; Day 9–10: 0.01 mg/kg q12h
doses.comments: DART regimen — total course 10 days. Discuss benefits vs neurodevelopmental risks with family.

drugs.monitoring: Glucose, BP, growth, infection signs, GI bleeding signs, CNS exam (neurodevelopmental risk in preterm).
drugs.adverse_effects: Hyperglycaemia, hypertension, GI perforation, growth suppression, adrenal suppression, increased infection risk, neurodevelopmental impairment (high-dose, early treatment), HCM (chronic high dose).
drugs.contraindications: Active untreated infection (sepsis), early use (< 1 week PNA, high dose) — increased CP risk.

review.notes: Avoid early high-dose dexamethasone (< 1 week PNA, > 0.5 mg/kg/day) — neurodevelopmental risk. Hydrocortisone is alternative for early adrenal insufficiency.

india_formulations.notes: 200 mg/kg buccal massage (≥ 35 wk and ≤ 48 h old) for transient hypoglycaemia.

doses.frequency: May repeat × up to 6 doses over 48 h

doses.comments: Titrate to maintain blood glucose in target range. Do not stop abruptly — rebound hypoglycaemia.

drugs.monitoring: Capillary blood glucose, urine glucose if GIR > 12 mg/kg/min.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hyperglycaemia, osmotic diuresis, hyperosmolar dehydration, IV irritation (concentrations > 12.5 % via central line only).
drugs.incompatibilities: 10 % is peripherally tolerated; ≥ 12.5 % needs central access.

review.notes: GIR mg/kg/min = (% dex × rate mL/hr × 0.167) / wt(kg). PediAid has a GIR calculator.

doses.comments: Adjust to glucose targets. If no response in 2–3 weeks, discontinue.

drugs.monitoring: Blood glucose, BP, fluid balance, CBC, electrolytes.
drugs.adverse_effects: Fluid retention (often co-administered with chlorothiazide), hypertrichosis, hyperuricaemia, neutropenia, pulmonary hypertension (case reports), GI upset.
drugs.contraindications: Hypersensitivity (often cross-reactive with sulfa); thiazide allergy (relative).

review.notes: Co-administer chlorothiazide for fluid retention.

doses.indication: HIV treatment (legacy regimens only — rarely first-line now)
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 100 mg/m²/dose (manufacturer) ; 50 mg/m²/dose may be more appropriate for neonates (guideline)

drugs.monitoring: LFTs (lactic acidosis with steatosis), pancreatic enzymes, peripheral neuropathy signs.
drugs.adverse_effects: Pancreatitis, peripheral neuropathy, lactic acidosis with hepatic steatosis (rare in neonates), retinal changes.
drugs.black_box_warning: Fatal lactic acidosis and hepatomegaly with steatosis; pancreatitis (especially with concurrent stavudine).

review.notes: Largely abandoned in current ART regimens — included for completeness.

doses.indication: CHF / SVT / atrial flutter — total digitalising dose (TDD)
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: TDD 15–25 mcg/kg IV (or 20–30 mcg/kg PO); divided 50% / 25% / 25% over 24 h

drugs.monitoring: ECG (PR prolongation, AV block, junctional rhythm), serum digoxin level (target 0.5–2 ng/mL), K, Mg, Ca, RFT.
drugs.adverse_effects: Toxicity — bradycardia, AV block, vomiting, lethargy, ventricular arrhythmia. Hypokalaemia and hypomagnesaemia potentiate toxicity.
drugs.contraindications: VF, idiopathic hypertrophic subaortic stenosis, AV block (without pacemaker), digitalis intoxication.
drugs.incompatibilities: Many — give IV slowly over 5 min.

review.notes: IV TDD = 75% of oral TDD. Very narrow therapeutic index — measure level at 6 h post-dose.

doses.indication: Acute digoxin overdose — known ingested amount
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Vials = (mg digoxin ingested × bioavailability) / 0.5 mg per vial. Bioavailability = 0.85 (solution), 0.8 (tablet). Each 40 mg vial binds ~0.5 mg digoxin.

drugs.monitoring: Continuous ECG, K, response, serum digoxin (will be falsely high after Fab — total but not free).
drugs.adverse_effects: Withdrawal of digitalis effect (worsening CHF, AF), allergic reaction (rare with Fab fragments), hypokalaemia → hyperkalaemia shifts.

review.notes: Rare in NICU. Document the digoxin level and timing of ingestion before dosing.

doses.comments: Begin at low dose; titrate. Useful when myocardial dysfunction with preserved BP.

drugs.monitoring: HR, BP, ECG, urine output, lactate.
drugs.adverse_effects: Tachycardia, ectopy, mild hypotension at higher doses, increased myocardial O₂ demand, hypokalaemia.
drugs.incompatibilities: Do NOT mix in same line as alkaline solutions (NaHCO₃) — degrades.

review.notes: PediAid Emergency NICU/PICU drug calculator handles dilution prep.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_min_range: 2–20 mcg/kg/min ; for fluid-refractory shock start < 10 mcg/kg/min
doses.comments: Dose-dependent: low (2–5 mcg/kg/min) — renal/dopaminergic ; medium (5–10) — β1 inotrope ; high (> 10) — α1 vasoconstrictor.

drugs.monitoring: HR, BP (continuous if available), ECG, urine output, perfusion, IV site for infiltration.
drugs.adverse_effects: Tachyarrhythmia, hypertension, peripheral / mesenteric / renal vasoconstriction at high doses, IV infiltration → tissue necrosis.
drugs.black_box_warning: Tissue sloughing on IV infiltration — infiltrate area with phentolamine 1 mg/mL (1–5 mL).
drugs.incompatibilities: Do NOT mix with alkaline solutions; ampicillin; sodium bicarbonate.

doses.indication: Persistent atelectasis / mucus plugging (off-label in NICU)
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 1.25–2.5 mL via nebulizer ; OR 0.2 mL/kg instilled directly into ETT

drugs.monitoring: Resolution of atelectasis, oxygenation, secretion clearance.
drugs.adverse_effects: Bronchospasm (rare), voice change.

review.notes: Off-label in NICU — most evidence is from cystic fibrosis.

drugs.monitoring: LFTs.
drugs.adverse_effects: Tooth discolouration (less common with doxycycline than other tetracyclines, but still avoid prolonged courses), photosensitivity, GI upset.
drugs.contraindications: Tetracycline hypersensitivity. Routine use in children < 8 years AVOIDED except for life-threatening infections (anthrax, RMSF).

review.notes: Reserved for life-threatening indications in neonates.

drugs.monitoring: Serum electrolytes, ionised Ca, P, Mg, ABG, urine output, weight.
drugs.adverse_effects: Imbalance — hypo/hyperkalaemia, hypo/hyperNa, calcium-phosphate precipitation in PN bag.

review.notes: Reference table only — not a single drug entry. Use alongside individual electrolyte entries.

doses.comments: Test for HBV before / during initiation — if HBV+, may flare on FTC withdrawal.

drugs.monitoring: HIV viral load, LFTs, RFT, HBsAg.
drugs.adverse_effects: Headache, nausea, hyperpigmentation (rare in neonates), lactic acidosis (rare).

doses.frequency: every 24 hours initially; may up-titrate to every 6 hours
doses.comments: Adjust by response. Increase every few days.

drugs.monitoring: BP, K, Cr, urine output.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypotension (especially first dose), hyperkalaemia, AKI / oliguria, neutropenia, cough (rare in infants).
drugs.contraindications: Bilateral renal artery stenosis, anuria, prior angioedema.

review.notes: Always start with a test dose. Significant Cr drops reported in preterm — caution.

drugs.category: ACE inhibitor — IV form of enalapril

doses.comments: Use IV form when oral not feasible. Titrate carefully — neonates are sensitive.

drugs.monitoring: BP, K, Cr.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypotension, hyperkalaemia, AKI.
drugs.contraindications: Bilateral renal artery stenosis, anuria, angioedema history.

doses.comments: Adjust to anti-Xa 0.5–1.0 U/mL (4 h post-dose).

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 2 mg/kg/dose initial (range 0.8–3 mg/kg q12h to maintain target)

doses.comments: Target anti-Xa 0.1–0.4 U/mL.

drugs.monitoring: Anti-factor Xa level 4 h post-dose ; CBC; signs of bleeding; renal function.
drugs.adverse_effects: Bleeding, heparin-induced thrombocytopenia (HIT — rare with LMWH vs UFH), hyperkalaemia, transaminitis, injection-site haematoma.
drugs.contraindications: Active major bleeding, severe thrombocytopenia, heparin / pork hypersensitivity, recent CNS / ocular surgery, recent IVH.

review.notes: Always evaluate for IVH before starting in preterm. PT, aPTT, platelets, renal function at baseline.

doses.indication: Anaemia of prematurity (limited evidence — not routinely recommended)
doses.comments: Always co-administer iron 2–6 mg/kg/day. Most meta-analyses show no clinically significant transfusion reduction.

drugs.monitoring: Hb, reticulocytes, iron status, BP.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypertension, polycythaemia, pure red cell aplasia (rare).

review.notes: Routine use NOT recommended — neither early nor late. May be considered for Jehovah's Witness families or specific situations.

doses.indication: Pertussis (only if azithromycin unavailable / aged > 1 mo)
Severe infection — IV (when oral not possible)
doses.comments: DO NOT give IM.

drugs.monitoring: Watch for hypertrophic pyloric stenosis (especially under 6 weeks); QT.
drugs.adverse_effects: HYPERTROPHIC PYLORIC STENOSIS (KIDs list — high risk in neonates < 6 weeks), QT prolongation, GI cramping.

review.notes: KIDs list — avoid in neonates UNLESS specific indication (chlamydia, pertussis, prokinetic for feeding intolerance).

drugs.monitoring: Continuous BP, HR, ECG.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypotension, bradycardia, bronchospasm (rare in selective β1), IV-site irritation.
drugs.contraindications: Decompensated heart failure, sinus bradycardia, AV block, hypersensitivity.

review.notes: Half-life ~9 min — easy to titrate quickly.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Individualised — 1 IU/kg raises FIX level by ~ 0.6 % in children < 6 yr
doses.comments: Required dose IU = wt(kg) × desired level rise (%) / 0.6. Use the in-product PK if available.

drugs.monitoring: FIX activity (target 80–100 % for major bleed, 30–50 % for minor), inhibitor titres.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypersensitivity, inhibitor development (~3–5%), thromboembolism (high doses).

review.notes: Initiate only with paediatric haematology team.

doses.indication: Congenital haemophilia A or B with inhibitors — acute bleed
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 90 mcg/kg/dose (higher 120 mcg/kg may be needed in children)
doses.frequency: every 2 hours until haemostasis achieved or treatment failure

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 90 mcg/kg/dose immediately before surgery; every 2 h during surgery; then every 2 h × 48 h, then every 2–6 h until healing

drugs.monitoring: Bleeding control; fibrinogen; ECG / D-dimer (thromboembolism risk).
drugs.adverse_effects: Thromboembolism (DVT, MI, stroke — black-box), hypersensitivity.
drugs.black_box_warning: Serious thrombotic events when used outside labelled indications. Use only by experienced clinicians.

review.notes: Off-label rescue use in massive haemorrhage is sometimes employed — consider thrombosis risk carefully.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Calculate to raise FX to 70–90 IU/dL
Repeat as needed to maintain FX ≥ 50 IU/dL until risk of bleeding resolves

drugs.monitoring: FX activity (peak and trough), bleeding signs.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypersensitivity, thromboembolism.

doses.comments: Adjust by Berichrom Activity Assay: +5 IU/kg if trough < 5 % ; -5 IU/kg for 2 troughs > 20 %. Children may need shorter intervals due to faster clearance.

drugs.monitoring: FXIII activity trough (target 5–20 %), bleeding signs.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypersensitivity, thromboembolism (rare).

drugs.monitoring: RFT.
drugs.adverse_effects: Headache, dizziness, mild thrombocytopenia (rare); accumulation in renal impairment.
drugs.renal_adjustment: Reduce dose / extend interval if Cr clearance < 50 mL/min.

review.notes: Preferred H₂RA in NICU after ranitidine withdrawal (NDMA). PPIs are an alternative for severe GERD.

doses.comments: Tolerance develops within days.
Rapid push > 5 mcg/kg may cause chest-wall rigidity — paralysis required.

drugs.monitoring: Continuous HR, RR, SpO₂; pain/sedation score; bowel sounds.
drugs.adverse_effects: Respiratory depression, chest-wall rigidity (rapid push), hypotension, bradycardia, urinary retention, ileus, tolerance, neonatal abstinence on weaning > 5 days.
drugs.contraindications: Severe respiratory depression without ventilation support.
drugs.black_box_warning: Risk of addiction, abuse, misuse. Life-threatening respiratory depression. Concomitant benzodiazepines or CNS depressants → profound sedation, respiratory depression, coma, death.

review.notes: Always have naloxone available bedside.

drugs.category: Oral iron — anaemia of prematurity prevention

doses.comments: Start by 2 weeks of age. ELBW (< 1000 g) may need 4 mg/kg/day.

drugs.monitoring: Hb, ferritin, transferrin saturation.
drugs.adverse_effects: GI upset, constipation, dark stools, tooth discolouration (rinse mouth), oxidative stress at high doses.

review.notes: Formula-fed term infants get adequate iron from formula. Breast-fed term: start by 4–6 months.

doses.comments: Steady state at 3–5 days. Do NOT increase more often than every 4 days. Correct K and Mg first. Check ECG and trough flecainide level (target 0.2–1 mcg/mL).

drugs.monitoring: Continuous ECG (QRS widening > 25% — proarrhythmic), serum level, K, Mg, RFT.
drugs.adverse_effects: Proarrhythmia (QRS widening, torsades), negative inotropy, dizziness, visual disturbance.
drugs.contraindications: Structural heart disease (especially post-MI), 2nd–3rd degree AV block, sick sinus.

review.notes: Initiate only with paediatric cardiology input.

doses.duration: 2 weeks after blood culture clearance and symptom resolution

doses.indication: Invasive candidiasis prophylaxis (BW < 1000 g, or < 1500 g in high-incidence units)

drugs.monitoring: LFTs, RFT.
drugs.adverse_effects: Transaminitis, GI upset, rash, rare QT prolongation.
drugs.contraindications: Concurrent QT-prolonging drugs (relative); known azole hypersensitivity.

review.notes: Higher dose (12–25 mg/kg load) used in critically ill / ECMO.

doses.comments: Increase interval if renal dysfunction.

drugs.monitoring: CBC (myelosuppression), LFTs, RFT, drug level (peak 50–100 mcg/mL, trough 25–50; toxic > 100).
drugs.adverse_effects: Bone marrow suppression (dose-related, reversible), hepatotoxicity, GI upset.
drugs.contraindications: Renal impairment without level monitoring; severe hepatic impairment.

review.notes: Always combined with amphotericin B for CNS fungal disease.

doses.frequency: May repeat every 45 sec until awake
doses.max_per_dose: Cumulative max 50 mcg/kg or 1 mg, whichever is smaller (limited neonatal data)

doses.route: IN (divided across nostrils via TB syringe)

drugs.monitoring: RR, SpO₂, sedation level, seizure (especially in chronic benzo / TCA exposure).
drugs.adverse_effects: Seizures (especially with mixed overdose / TCA / chronic benzo dependence), arrhythmia, re-sedation as flumazenil clears.
drugs.contraindications: TCA overdose, chronic benzo dependence (precipitates withdrawal seizures), known hypersensitivity.

review.notes: Half-life shorter than most benzos — re-sedation possible. Continue monitoring 2 hours post-reversal.

india_formulations.strength: 0.4 mg, 1 mg, 5 mg tablet; 0.4 mg/mL drops

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 56 mcg/kg/day (max 140 mcg/day for ≥ 3 kg ; 91 mcg/day for 1–3 kg ; 42 mcg/day for < 1 kg)

doses.indication: Maternal folate deficiency, breast-fed infant supplement

drugs.monitoring: Routine clinical; CBC if deficient.
drugs.adverse_effects: None at usual doses.

cross_checks.source: AAP — Iron / vitamin supplementation in preterm

doses.route: IV (or IM in adults; IV preferred in paediatrics)
doses.comments: Dosed in PHENYTOIN EQUIVALENTS (PE). 1 mg PE = 1 mg phenytoin sodium.

drugs.monitoring: Continuous ECG + BP during loading. Free phenytoin level (target 1–2 mcg/mL).
drugs.adverse_effects: Same as phenytoin (cardiac, dermatologic, hepatic, blood dyscrasia) BUT less infusion-site phlebitis / extravasation injury. Pruritus / paraesthesia at infusion sites is common.

review.notes: Always order in PE. Compatible with D5W and NS — unlike phenytoin.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 1 mg/kg/dose initial; may increase to 2 mg/kg IV or 6 mg/kg PO

doses.frequency: every 12 hours initially (every 6–8 h if > 1 month)
doses.comments: Consider alternate-day therapy for long-term use.

drugs.monitoring: Electrolytes (Na, K, Cl, HCO₃), BUN/Cr, weight, urine output, hearing if prolonged or in renal impairment.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypokalaemia, hyponatraemia, hypochloraemic alkalosis, hypocalcaemia, hypomagnesaemia, ototoxicity (high dose, fast push), nephrocalcinosis (with prolonged therapy), PDA.
drugs.black_box_warning: Profound diuresis with water + electrolyte depletion if excessive doses — careful clinical supervision required.

review.notes: Consider furosemide infusion 0.05–0.4 mg/kg/h for refractory volume overload.

doses.duration: ≥ 6 weeks (longer in some cases). Switch to oral valganciclovir when feasible.
doses.comments: Reduce dose by 50% for ANC < 500.

drugs.monitoring: CBC weekly (neutropenia, thrombocytopenia), LFTs, RFT, viral load, audiology.
drugs.adverse_effects: Myelosuppression (neutropenia in 60–80%), nephrotoxicity, hepatotoxicity, IV-site phlebitis, mutagenicity / carcinogenicity (animal data).
drugs.black_box_warning: Granulocytopenia, anaemia, thrombocytopenia, mutagenic and carcinogenic potential, possible impairment of fertility. Pregnancy category C.

review.notes: Valganciclovir 16 mg/kg q12h PO is preferred over IV ganciclovir for the entire 6-month course in symptomatic congenital CMV (Kimberlin 2015 NEJM).

india_formulations.notes: For NICU use, dilute 40 mg/mL stock to 10 mg/mL or 2 mg/mL before use to minimise error.
Preferred NICU strength when available. Confirm vial label.

doses.ga_band: Preterm and Term — see PMA bands below
doses.comments: Extended-interval gives Cmax ~9–11 mg/L and Cmin < 1 mg/L. Source: Dersch-Mills 2012/2016.

doses.pma_band: ≤ 29 weeks (or significant asphyxia / PDA / on indomethacin)
doses.frequency: every 48 h / every 36 h / every 24 h (corresponding to age bands)
doses.comments: Reduce to longer interval at greater immaturity.
Clearance reduced during cooling — interval extended. Day 5 (rewarmed) clearance is ~29% higher; revert to standard interval.

drugs.administration: Infuse over 30–120 min via dedicated line. Concentration 2 mg/mL or 10 mg/mL. NEVER mix in same line/syringe with penicillins (β-lactams inactivate gentamicin in vitro). IM injection has variable absorption in small infants — IV preferred.
drugs.monitoring: Therapeutic levels — Peak (30 min post end of infusion): 5–12 mg/L (extended-interval target 8–13 mg/L). Trough (just pre-next-dose): ≤ 1 mg/L (some recommend < 0.5). Measure if treating > 48 h, on inotropes, fluid status changing, or in renal impairment. Routine levels NOT mandatory in low-risk full-term neonates with normal urine output.
drugs.adverse_effects: Reversible renal tubular dysfunction (raised urinary Na/Ca/Mg). Vestibular and auditory ototoxicity (often irreversible — black-box warning). Increased risk with concurrent furosemide, vancomycin, or hypermagnesaemia. Neuromuscular blockade with pancuronium. Gentamicin ointment for ocular prophylaxis has been linked to periocular ulcerative dermatitis — avoid in neonates (KIDs list).
drugs.contraindications: Aminoglycoside hypersensitivity. Caution in renal impairment, dehydration, concurrent ototoxic drugs.
drugs.renal_adjustment: PMA-based dosing already accounts for maturation. In overt AKI or oliguria, hold and re-dose by serum level.
drugs.incompatibilities: Do NOT mix in same line with: amphotericin B, ampicillin, azithromycin, furosemide, imipenem/cilastatin, heparin > 1 U/mL, indomethacin, nafcillin, oxacillin, propofol. Compatible: acyclovir, caffeine citrate, cefepime, ceftazidime, dopamine, fluconazole, magnesium sulfate, milrinone, morphine.
drugs.reconstitution: 10 mg/mL pre-mixed solution available — no reconstitution needed. Dilute 40 mg/mL adult stock if using.
drugs.black_box_warning: Aminoglycoside therapy carries risk of nephrotoxicity, ototoxicity (often irreversible), and neurotoxicity. Higher risk with renal impairment, dehydration, high dose or prolonged therapy. Discontinue or adjust if toxicity appears.

cross_checks.source: WHO Model Formulary for Children 2024 — gentamicin
cross_checks.agreement: agrees on 5 mg/kg q24h preterm/term ≥ 35 wk; longer intervals at lower PMA
primary source for the 22-h sampling tables

review.notes: Therapeutic drug monitoring is best practice — prefer audiology screen at discharge for any neonate exposed > 5 days.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_min_range: 10–20 mcg/kg/hr (or 0.5–1 mg/day total regardless of weight)
doses.comments: Glucose rise expected within 1 hour.

drugs.monitoring: Capillary glucose, electrolytes, ammonia.
drugs.adverse_effects: Vomiting, hypokalaemia, rebound hypoglycaemia, paradoxical hypoglycaemia in glycogen-depleted neonates.

review.notes: Useful while securing IV access for D10 bolus. Not a substitute for ongoing dextrose infusion.

drugs.category: Anticoagulant — direct thrombin / factor Xa via antithrombin

doses.comments: Continuous infusion thereafter, target ACT 180–220 s.
Target anti-Xa 0.35–0.7 U/mL (or aPTT 60–85 s). Treatment course 10–14 days; consider switch to LMWH after 3–5 days. Renal vein thrombosis: 6 weeks–3 months total.

doses.indication: Maintaining patency of central vascular catheters

drugs.monitoring: ACT (ECMO), aPTT or anti-Xa level, platelet count (HIT screen at day 4 and weekly), bleeding signs, IVH on cranial USG (preterm).
drugs.adverse_effects: Bleeding, heparin-induced thrombocytopenia (HIT, type II — rare in neonates), hyperkalaemia, transaminitis, osteoporosis (chronic).
drugs.contraindications: Active major bleeding, severe thrombocytopenia, recent IVH, recent CNS surgery.
drugs.incompatibilities: Many — use a dedicated line.

doses.indication: Prevention of perinatal HBV transmission — HBsAg-positive mother
doses.route: IM (anterolateral thigh; different site from vaccine)
doses.frequency: Single dose within 12 h of birth + first hepatitis B vaccine dose at the same visit (different site)

doses.indication: Prevention of perinatal HBV — HBsAg-unknown mother, BW < 2 kg
doses.frequency: Within 12 h of birth + HepB vaccine

doses.indication: Prevention of perinatal HBV — HBsAg-unknown mother, BW ≥ 2 kg
doses.frequency: Only if mother tests positive — give as soon as known, no later than day 7

doses.indication: When HepB vaccine series is delayed
doses.frequency: Repeat at 3 months if HepB vaccination is delayed; further dose at 6 months if vaccination is refused

drugs.monitoring: HepB serology at 9–12 months postvaccination (anti-HBs titre).
drugs.adverse_effects: Local injection-site reactions; rare systemic hypersensitivity.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Engerix-B 10 mcg (0.5 mL) OR Recombivax HB 5 mcg (0.5 mL)
doses.comments: BW < 2 kg: give 4 total doses (birth + 1, 2–3, 6 months).

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Engerix-B 10 mcg (0.5 mL) OR Recombivax HB 5 mcg (0.5 mL)
doses.frequency: Within 24 h of birth (if BW ≥ 2 kg) ; defer to 1 month chronological age (if BW < 2 kg)
doses.comments: Standard 3-dose series: 0–1–6 months for ≥ 2 kg.

drugs.monitoring: Anti-HBs titre at 9–12 months for high-risk infants.
drugs.adverse_effects: Local injection-site soreness, mild fever; very rare anaphylaxis.
drugs.contraindications: Severe hypersensitivity to vaccine components or yeast.

review.notes: Birth dose is universal in India per NIP, regardless of maternal status.

drugs.drug: Hib Conjugate / Hepatitis B Combination Vaccine

doses.comments: DO NOT give before 6 weeks of age. Premature infants vaccinated by chronological age (not corrected age). Some experts delay first dose in chronically ill preterm.

drugs.monitoring: Local reaction; vaccine response if antibody titres needed.
drugs.adverse_effects: Local injection-site reaction, mild fever; rare anaphylaxis.
drugs.contraindications: Severe reaction to prior dose; severe hypersensitivity to vaccine components.

review.notes: Pentavalent (DTwP-HepB-Hib) is the standard combination in India NIP.

doses.indication: IV / SC fluid extravasation (NOT for vasopressors — use phentolamine)
doses.route: Subcutaneous (5 separate 0.2 mL injections around periphery of extravasation site)
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 1 mL (= 150 units) total, divided into 5 SC injections of 0.2 mL each
doses.comments: Alternative: dilute 0.1 mL of 150 U/mL with 0.9 mL NS = 15 U/mL ; inject 5 × 0.2 mL. Use 25- or 26-gauge needle, change after each injection.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 150 U (range 50–300 U) before drug administration or added to injection

drugs.monitoring: Site healing, perfusion.
drugs.adverse_effects: Local reaction, rare anaphylaxis.

review.notes: First-line for IV fluid extravasation. Phentolamine for catecholamine extravasation; nitroglycerin for severe ischaemia.

drugs.monitoring: BP, HR (reflex tachycardia), urine output.
drugs.adverse_effects: Reflex tachycardia, hypotension, fluid retention; lupus-like syndrome (chronic).

review.notes: Often paired with a β-blocker to attenuate reflex tachycardia.

doses.comments: Distinct from chlorothiazide — do not confuse.

drugs.monitoring: Electrolytes (K, Na), urine output.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypokalaemia, hyponatraemia, hyperuricaemia, hypercalcaemia, hyperglycaemia.

review.notes: Often combined with spironolactone in BPD chronic care.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Physiologic 7–9 mg/m²/day in 2–3 doses ; salt-losing CAH may need higher mineralocorticoid (fludrocortisone)
doses.frequency: Adjust based on growth, electrolytes, BP

doses.indication: Refractory hypotension (vasopressor-resistant) / suspected adrenal insufficiency in shock
doses.comments: Use lowest effective dose; taper to minimise HPA suppression.

doses.indication: BPD prevention (alternative to dexamethasone, low-risk approach)
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 1 mg/kg/dose every 12 h × 7 d, then 0.5 mg/kg/dose every 12 h × 3 d (PREMILOC regimen)
doses.comments: 1 mg/kg/dose total daily for 7 days, taper × 3 days. PREMILOC trial showed BPD reduction without neurodevelopmental impairment.

drugs.monitoring: Glucose, BP, Na, K, growth.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hyperglycaemia, hypertension, GI perforation (especially with concurrent indomethacin / ibuprofen — separate by 24 h), HPA-axis suppression.

review.notes: Hydrocortisone preferred over dexamethasone for early BPD prevention in extreme preterm — fewer neurodevelopmental concerns.

doses.comments: A second course may be given.

drugs.monitoring: Renal function (Cr, urine output), platelets, GI bleeding signs.
drugs.adverse_effects: Renal impairment, oliguria, GI perforation, NEC, IVH (less than indomethacin), platelet dysfunction.
drugs.contraindications: Active bleeding, NEC, severe oliguria, thrombocytopenia (< 50k), hyperbilirubinaemia (relative — displaces from albumin).
drugs.black_box_warning: Cardiovascular thrombotic events, MI, stroke (NSAID class warning) — primarily applies to chronic adult use, not the short PDA course.

review.notes: Indomethacin and paracetamol (acetaminophen) are alternatives; ibuprofen has lower NEC and renal AE rates than indomethacin.

drugs.monitoring: Clinical response; CBC, RFT, neuro status (seizure risk).
drugs.adverse_effects: Diarrhoea, nausea, transaminitis, seizures (relatively higher than meropenem — caution in renal impairment / CNS infection), eosinophilia.

review.notes: Meropenem generally preferred over imipenem in CNS infections — lower seizure risk.

doses.indication: Isoimmune haemolytic disease — total bilirubin at/above escalation threshold
doses.frequency: May repeat at 12 h if needed

drugs.monitoring: BP during infusion, urine output (rare nephrotoxicity), Hb (haemolysis from anti-A/B isoagglutinins), thrombosis signs.
drugs.adverse_effects: Infusion reactions (slow infusion), aseptic meningitis, haemolysis (passive anti-A/B), thrombosis (uncommon in neonates), AKI (sucrose-containing brands).
drugs.contraindications: IgA deficiency with anti-IgA antibodies; severe systemic reaction to prior IVIG.

review.notes: Avoid sucrose-containing IVIG products in renal impairment / preterm.

doses.comments: Hold subsequent doses if anuria / severe oliguria / Cr rising.

doses.frequency: every 24 hours × 3 doses, started within 6–24 h of birth

drugs.monitoring: Renal function, urine output, platelets, ductal status (echo), bleeding, GI status.
drugs.adverse_effects: Renal impairment / oliguria (often transient), GI perforation, NEC, IVH, platelet dysfunction.
drugs.contraindications: Active bleeding, NEC, severe oliguria, thrombocytopenia, hyperbilirubinaemia (relative).
drugs.black_box_warning: NSAID class — CV thrombotic events; contraindicated in CABG. Renal effects in neonates can be substantial.

review.notes: Indian NICUs increasingly prefer ibuprofen or paracetamol over indomethacin due to lower NEC rates.

doses.comments: May need supplemental vitamin A for low BW infants.

doses.indication: PN supplementation — ≥ 3 kg up to 11 yr

drugs.monitoring: Routine clinical; vitamin levels if deficiency suspected.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypervitaminosis A or D (high dose / prolonged).

doses.comments: Use 1 U/mL or dilute as 0.1 U/mL for very low rates. Always run via a syringe pump.
Monitor glucose hourly; hypoglycaemia is the chief risk.
Basal 0.14–0.7 U/kg/day; titrate to capillary glucose.

drugs.monitoring: Hourly glucose during infusion changes; serum K (drops fast).
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypoglycaemia (life-threatening), hypokalaemia.
drugs.incompatibilities: Insulin adsorbs to plastic tubing — flush 20 mL of insulin solution through new tubing before connecting to the patient.

review.notes: Always specify 'human regular insulin' (not analogue) for neonatal IV use.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 75–175 mcg per dose via jet nebuliser

drugs.monitoring: RR, breath sounds, HR.
drugs.adverse_effects: Dry mouth, paradoxical bronchospasm (rare), urinary retention.

review.notes: Routinely combined with albuterol (DuoNeb) in older children, less commonly in NICU.

doses.indication: Iron supplementation when oral not tolerated / on EPO
doses.comments: Test dose before first full infusion (anaphylaxis risk). Iron sucrose is generally preferred over dextran due to lower anaphylaxis risk.

drugs.monitoring: Hb, ferritin, transferrin saturation; watch for anaphylaxis during test dose.
drugs.adverse_effects: Anaphylaxis (TEST DOSE mandatory), arthralgia, hypotension, transaminitis.
drugs.contraindications: Anaemia not due to iron deficiency, prior anaphylaxis.
drugs.black_box_warning: Anaphylactic-type reactions including fatalities have occurred. A test dose is mandatory before each course.

review.notes: Iron sucrose / ferric carboxymaltose preferred over iron dextran in modern NICU.

doses.indication: Symptomatic bradycardia / heart block (when pacing unavailable)
doses.comments: Titrate to HR. Correct acidosis first.

drugs.monitoring: Continuous ECG + HR + BP, urine output.
drugs.adverse_effects: Tachyarrhythmia, hypotension (β2-mediated vasodilation), hyperglycaemia, increased myocardial oxygen demand.

review.notes: Largely replaced by transcutaneous / transvenous pacing.

doses.comments: Test for HBV first — risk of HBV flare on withdrawal.

drugs.monitoring: HIV viral load, LFTs, CBC, HBsAg.
drugs.adverse_effects: Headache, GI upset, lactic acidosis (rare), pancreatitis (rare).

drugs.monitoring: Watch for infection (NEC, pneumonia).
drugs.adverse_effects: Same class effects as pantoprazole — increased C. diff, NEC, pneumonia, hypomagnesaemia (chronic).

review.notes: Use shortest course possible — long-term PPI in preterm has been associated with NEC / sepsis.

doses.loading_dose_per_kg: 40 mg/kg load (may repeat with 20 mg/kg if needed)
doses.comments: Consider after phenobarbital fails or instead of phenytoin in cardiac instability.

drugs.monitoring: Seizure response. Levels not routinely required (target 12–46 mcg/mL if measured).
drugs.adverse_effects: Sedation, irritability, behavioural changes (older children). Generally well tolerated in neonates.
drugs.renal_adjustment: Reduce dose / extend interval — primarily renally cleared.

review.notes: Increasingly used as 1st- or 2nd-line for neonatal seizures (favourable cardiac safety vs phenytoin). NEOLEV2: phenobarbital 80% vs levetiracetam 28% efficacy — phenobarb still preferred 1st-line in most guidelines.

doses.comments: Crush tablet, mix with small volume of water/breast milk. Do NOT mix with soy formula or iron — reduces absorption.

drugs.monitoring: TSH and free T4 at 2 weeks, then every 1–3 months for first year. Target free T4 in upper half of reference range, TSH 0.5–2 mU/L.
drugs.adverse_effects: Tachycardia, irritability, poor weight gain (overdose); persistent symptoms, lethargy (underdose). Craniosynostosis (chronic overdose).

cross_checks.source: AAP / Indian Society of Paediatric Endocrinology

review.notes: Always start as soon as congenital hypothyroidism is confirmed by repeat TSH. Treatment delay > 2 weeks risks neurodevelopmental impairment.

doses.comments: Maintenance: 10–50 mcg/kg/min IV continuous (preterm — lowest dose).

drugs.monitoring: Continuous ECG, BP, neuro status; serum lidocaine level (target 1.5–5 mcg/mL; toxic > 5).
drugs.adverse_effects: CNS toxicity (drowsiness, twitching, seizure), AV block, hypotension, methaemoglobinaemia (rare).
drugs.contraindications: Stokes-Adams syndrome, severe sinoatrial / AV block.

review.notes: Amiodarone is preferred over lidocaine in PALS 2020 for paediatric pulseless VT/VF.

doses.indication: Refractory neonatal seizure (3rd-line, when phenobarb + phenytoin fail)
doses.dose_per_kg_per_min_range: Maintenance: 6 mg/kg/hr × 6 h, then 4 mg/kg/hr × 12 h, then 2 mg/kg/hr × 12 h (taper)
doses.comments: Total ~ 30 hours. Avoid concurrent phenytoin (combined Na-channel toxicity).

drugs.monitoring: Continuous EEG (seizure response), ECG (arrhythmia, QT), BP.
drugs.adverse_effects: Cardiac arrhythmia (proarrhythmic at high levels), CNS toxicity, methaemoglobinaemia.
drugs.contraindications: Concurrent phenytoin (relative — avoid combination); severe AV block; preterm or hypothermia (clearance reduced — risk of accumulation).

review.notes: Use protocol-driven taper — abrupt stop NOT recommended.

doses.indication: Topical analgesia for procedures (heel prick, IV, lumbar puncture)
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Max 1 g over max 10 cm² for max 1 hour
doses.comments: Methaemoglobinaemia risk in infants < 12 months on methaemoglobin-inducing drugs — AVOID.

drugs.monitoring: SpO₂, methaemoglobin if cyanosis, local reaction.
drugs.adverse_effects: Methaemoglobinaemia (especially preterm or with concurrent inducing drugs), local pallor / erythema, blanching.
drugs.contraindications: Preterm < 37 weeks, methaemoglobin-inducing drug exposure, hypersensitivity.

review.notes: Avoid in preterm. Sucrose + non-nutritive sucking is a safer alternative for routine procedural pain.

drugs.monitoring: CBC weekly (myelosuppression), lactate (if metabolic acidosis suspected), neuro status (peripheral / optic neuropathy with prolonged use).
drugs.adverse_effects: Myelosuppression (especially > 14 days — thrombocytopenia, anaemia), serotonin syndrome with serotonergic drugs, lactic acidosis, peripheral / optic neuropathy.
drugs.contraindications: MAOI use (within 2 weeks); concomitant serotonergic drugs (relative).

review.notes: Limit to MRSA / VRE infection where standard β-lactams or vancomycin can't be used.

doses.loading_dose_per_kg: Start 1 g/kg/day; advance by 0.5–1 g/kg/day to goal 3 g/kg/day
doses.comments: 20% emulsion is preferred over 10% (less plasma triglyceride). SMOFlipid / mixed-oil emulsions reduce PNALD risk vs pure soybean Intralipid.

drugs.monitoring: Serum triglycerides (target < 250 mg/dL preterm, < 400 term), bilirubin, LFTs.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypertriglyceridaemia, cholestasis (PNALD with prolonged use), allergic reactions, sepsis with line contamination.

review.notes: SMOFlipid (mixed-oil) preferred over pure soybean Intralipid in preterm requiring PN > 7–14 days.

drugs.monitoring: Renal function, K, Mg, LFTs, CBC.
drugs.adverse_effects: Less nephrotoxic than conventional ampho. Infusion reactions, electrolyte losses, hepatotoxicity.

review.notes: Liposomal preferred over conventional / lipid-complex when renal function is precarious.

india_formulations.strength: Lopinavir 80 mg / Ritonavir 20 mg per mL
india_formulations.notes: Solution contains 42.4% v/v ETHANOL and 15.3% propylene glycol — DO NOT USE in PMA < 42 weeks.

doses.ga_band: PMA ≥ 42 weeks AND postnatal age ≥ 14 days
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Lopinavir 16 mg/kg + Ritonavir 4 mg/kg (or LPV 300 mg/m² + RTV 75 mg/m²)
doses.comments: DO NOT use once-daily dosing in paediatrics. DO NOT co-administer with efavirenz / nevirapine / nelfinavir under 6 months.

drugs.monitoring: Glucose, lipids, LFTs, lactic acidosis, ECG (QT prolongation, AV block), drug interactions (powerful CYP3A4 inhibitor).
drugs.adverse_effects: Diarrhoea, hyperglycaemia, hyperlipidaemia, transaminitis, prolonged QT/PR, ethanol/propylene glycol toxicity in preterm (oral solution).
drugs.contraindications: PMA < 42 weeks (oral solution toxicity); PR > 0.2 sec or QT prolongation; concurrent CYP3A-sensitive drugs.
drugs.black_box_warning: Cardiac, lactic acidosis, hepatic toxicity. Risk of toxicity from ethanol / propylene glycol in oral solution — DO NOT use in neonates < 14 days postnatal age or PMA < 42 weeks.

review.notes: Discuss with paediatric HIV specialist. India NACO programme uses ART per WHO guidelines.

drugs.monitoring: RR, SpO₂, HR (respiratory depression), level of consciousness.
drugs.adverse_effects: Respiratory depression, hypotension, paradoxical agitation, sedation.
drugs.contraindications: Acute narrow-angle glaucoma; severe respiratory insufficiency without ventilation; sleep apnoea.
drugs.black_box_warning: Concomitant use with opioids → profound sedation, respiratory depression, coma, death. Risk of dependence and withdrawal.

review.notes: Injection contains benzyl alcohol — caution in preterm < 1500 g (gasping syndrome).

doses.route: Intratracheal (4 equal aliquots, infant repositioned between)
doses.frequency: Up to 4 doses in first 48 h, no more frequently than every 6–12 h
doses.comments: First dose within 10–30 min of birth (after initial resuscitation).

drugs.monitoring: Continuous SpO₂, HR, chest expansion.
drugs.adverse_effects: Transient bradycardia, desaturation, ETT block.

review.notes: Synthetic surfactant — withdrawn from US market in 2015. Poractant alfa is the standard NICU surfactant in India.

doses.route: IV / IO rapid push (over several minutes)

drugs.monitoring: ECG (QT, AV block), DTRs, RR, BP, serum Mg, calcium, renal function.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypotension, bradycardia, respiratory depression, hyporeflexia, hypocalcaemia.
drugs.contraindications: Heart block, severe renal impairment without level monitoring.
drugs.incompatibilities: Calcium-containing solutions form precipitate.

review.notes: 1 g MgSO₄ = ~8 mEq Mg = ~98 mg elemental Mg. Always document salt vs elemental.

doses.comments: Correct fluid + electrolyte imbalances first. Caution in renal impairment.

drugs.monitoring: Serum osmolality (target ≤ 320 mOsm/kg), urine output, electrolytes (Na, K), RFT, ICP if monitored.
drugs.adverse_effects: Volume overload (initial), then dehydration / hyperosmolar AKI, rebound ICP, electrolyte derangements.
drugs.contraindications: Anuria, severe pulmonary oedema, intracranial bleed (relative — discuss with neurosurgery).

review.notes: Hypertonic 3% saline is the preferred osmotherapy in many neonatal centres now.

doses.indication: Caloric supplementation in cholestasis / chylothorax / fat malabsorption
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Mix into formula or feed; titrate by tolerance and weight gain (no fixed dose)
doses.comments: Provides ~ 8.3 kcal/g. Mixes easily with formula. Begin with small amount and increase as tolerated.

drugs.monitoring: Weight gain, GI tolerance, LFTs.
drugs.adverse_effects: Diarrhoea, vomiting, ketosis at high doses.

doses.frequency: every 8–12 hours by GA / postnatal age band

drugs.monitoring: Clinical response; CBC, RFT, LFTs.
drugs.adverse_effects: Diarrhoea, eosinophilia, transaminitis, rare seizures (especially in renal impairment), thrombocytosis.

review.notes: Reserve for ESBL or critically ill MDR sepsis; use stewardship.

doses.comments: Adjust by 10–20% based on Finnegan score. Standardised stepwise wean (8 steps over ~16 days).

drugs.monitoring: Finnegan / NAS score, RR, SpO₂, ECG (QT prolongation), feeding pattern.
drugs.adverse_effects: Respiratory depression, sedation, prolonged QT, NAS rebound on rapid wean.
drugs.contraindications: Severe respiratory depression without ventilation; concurrent serotonergic drugs (relative — serotonin syndrome).
drugs.black_box_warning: Respiratory depression, addiction, QT prolongation. Use only by experienced practitioners.

review.notes: Methadone has a long, variable half-life — close monitoring during titration. Morphine and buprenorphine are alternatives with shorter NICU stays.

doses.indication: Feeding intolerance / GERD (LIMITED ROLE in neonates)
doses.comments: TABLET form NOT recommended in paeds — methaemoglobinaemia + tardive dyskinesia risk. Avoid prolonged use.

drugs.monitoring: Watch for extrapyramidal signs (especially in renal impairment / accumulation), methaemoglobinaemia (cyanosis).
drugs.adverse_effects: Extrapyramidal symptoms (acute dystonia, oculogyric crisis), tardive dyskinesia (chronic), methaemoglobinaemia.
drugs.black_box_warning: Tardive dyskinesia with chronic use (> 12 weeks). Can be irreversible.

review.notes: Erythromycin (10 mg/kg q6h) is preferred prokinetic in neonates — fewer EPS effects.

doses.frequency: PMA-based: ≤ 29 wk: q24h ; 30–36 wk: q12h ; 37–44 wk: q8h ; ≥ 45 wk: q6h

drugs.monitoring: Clinical response; CBC, LFTs.
drugs.adverse_effects: GI upset, peripheral neuropathy (chronic), seizures (rare), disulfiram-like reaction with ethanol.

drugs.monitoring: LFTs, CBC.
drugs.adverse_effects: Transaminitis, infusion reactions (rare), hyperbilirubinaemia (in neonates).

review.notes: Preferred echinocandin for neonates — most published PK / safety data.

drugs.category: Caloric supplement — oral / enteral fat emulsion

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Mix into formula or feed (4.5 kcal/mL)
doses.comments: 50 % safflower oil — used to supplement orally or added to enteral formula.

drugs.monitoring: Weight gain, GI tolerance, LFTs, triglycerides.
drugs.adverse_effects: GI upset.

india_formulations.strength: 2 mg/mL (Versed brand abroad; rare in India)

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: IV 0.05–0.15 mg/kg ; IN 0.2–0.3 mg/kg ; SL 0.2 mg/kg ; PO 0.25 mg/kg

doses.comments: Tolerance with prolonged use.

drugs.monitoring: RR, SpO₂, HR, BP, sedation level.
drugs.adverse_effects: Respiratory depression, hypotension (especially with opioids or rapid push), paradoxical agitation, withdrawal after prolonged use.
drugs.contraindications: Acute narrow-angle glaucoma; KIDs list — avoid in VLBW due to IVH/PVL/death risk.
drugs.black_box_warning: Respiratory depression / arrest — only use where continuous monitoring is available.

review.notes: Avoid in VLBW unless absolutely necessary; prefer fentanyl + dexmedetomidine or morphine in ELBW.
