doses.indication: Low cardiac output (post-cardiac surgery, PPHN, septic shock)
doses.loading_dose_per_kg: 50 mcg/kg over 15 min (or 75 mcg/kg over 60 min)
doses.comments: In premature < 30 wk GA, infuse load over 3 hours. Adjust by haemodynamic response.

drugs.monitoring: Continuous BP, HR, ECG, urine output, electrolytes (K, Mg).
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypotension (especially during load — pretreat with volume), arrhythmia, thrombocytopenia.
drugs.incompatibilities: Do NOT mix with furosemide — precipitate.

review.notes: Some neonatal units omit the loading dose to avoid hypotension.

doses.comments: Postoperative may need higher rates.
Wean by 10–20% every 2–3 days based on Finnegan score.
Reduced clearance during cooling — lower doses.

drugs.monitoring: RR, SpO₂, HR, BP, sedation/pain score, bowel sounds.
drugs.adverse_effects: Respiratory depression, hypotension, bradycardia, urinary retention, ileus, pruritus, tolerance, NAS on weaning > 5 days.
drugs.contraindications: Severe respiratory depression without ventilation support; concurrent MAOI (or within 14 days); severe GI obstruction.
drugs.black_box_warning: Respiratory depression — neonates highly sensitive. Interaction with benzodiazepines, alcohol, other CNS depressants → fatal respiratory depression.

review.notes: Always have naloxone bedside. PediAid Drug Calculator handles infusion prep.

drugs.category: Antibiotic — Fluoroquinolone (topical ophthalmic only in neonates)
drugs.monitoring: Resolution of conjunctivitis.
drugs.adverse_effects: Local irritation, transient blurred vision.

review.notes: Systemic fluoroquinolones AVOIDED in neonates due to cartilage concerns. Topical use is acceptable.

india_formulations.strength: Per 1 mL: Vit A 250 mcg RAE / D 400 IU / C 50 mg / E 5 mg / B-complex (varies by brand)
india_formulations.notes: Compositions vary by brand — verify each ingredient against requirement.

doses.indication: Routine supplementation — preterm and exclusively breastfed term

drugs.monitoring: Routine clinical.
drugs.adverse_effects: GI upset, hypervitaminosis (rare at recommended doses).

review.notes: Avoid duplicating with separate vitamin D / iron supplements.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Apply small amount to affected area

drugs.monitoring: Clinical response.
drugs.adverse_effects: Local irritation, rare allergic dermatitis.

review.notes: Watch for resistance with prolonged / repeated use.

drugs.monitoring: CBC, LFTs, RFT.
drugs.adverse_effects: Phlebitis (severe — must be diluted), interstitial nephritis, neutropenia, hypokalaemia, hepatotoxicity (less than oxacillin).
drugs.incompatibilities: Alkalinising agents.

review.notes: Less hepatotoxic than oxacillin. Cefazolin is alternative for MSSA.

doses.comments: ETT dose 2–3× IV dose. WARNING: KIDs list — avoid in postpartum resuscitation due to seizure risk.
Use lower dose to preserve analgesia in iatrogenic over-sedation.

drugs.monitoring: Continuous RR, SpO₂, HR, GCS (re-narcotisation possible — half-life of naloxone often shorter than the opioid).
drugs.adverse_effects: Withdrawal in opioid-tolerant infants (NAS / mother on opioids → seizure, vomiting, pulmonary oedema, cardiac arrest), hypertension, tachycardia.
drugs.contraindications: Maternal opioid use / chronic opioid exposure (relative — use lowest effective dose).

cross_checks.agreement: no longer recommended in delivery room as part of initial resuscitation

review.notes: Naloxone is no longer part of routine NRP in the delivery room — establish ventilation first.

doses.comments: ALWAYS pretreat with atropine 0.02 mg/kg or glycopyrrolate to block muscarinic effects.

drugs.monitoring: HR, RR, secretions, train-of-four, muscle tone.
drugs.adverse_effects: Bradycardia, bronchospasm, increased secretions, abdominal cramps, miosis.

review.notes: Sugammadex (where available) preferred for rocuronium / vecuronium reversal — neostigmine reverses any non-depolariser.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: PMA-based dosing similar to gentamicin — refer to local protocol
doses.comments: Less commonly used than gentamicin / amikacin globally.

drugs.monitoring: Therapeutic levels (peak 6–12 mg/L, trough < 2 mg/L), RFT, audiology.
drugs.adverse_effects: Nephrotoxicity, ototoxicity (perhaps slightly less than gentamicin).
drugs.black_box_warning: Aminoglycoside class — neurotoxicity, ototoxicity, nephrotoxicity.

review.notes: Rarely first-line — gentamicin / amikacin are usually preferred.

doses.indication: Perinatal HIV prophylaxis — BW 1.5 to 2 kg
doses.frequency: Dose 1: within 48 h of birth (ideally 6–12 h) ; Dose 2: 48 h after dose 1 ; Dose 3: 96 h after dose 2
doses.comments: Must be given with zidovudine.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 0–2 wk: 2 mg/kg BD ; 2–4 wk: 4 mg/kg BD ; 4–6 wk: 6 mg/kg BD

drugs.monitoring: LFTs, rash (Stevens-Johnson), CBC, HIV viral load.
drugs.adverse_effects: Severe hepatotoxicity (rarer in neonates), Stevens-Johnson syndrome / TEN, rash.
drugs.black_box_warning: Severe, life-threatening hepatotoxicity and serious skin reactions (SJS, TEN).

review.notes: Standard perinatal HIV prophylaxis in India: AZT + NVP combination from birth × 6 weeks.

doses.comments: Onset within minutes; full effect ~ 50 hours (adult data — neonatal kinetics may differ).

drugs.monitoring: Continuous BP, HR, ECG.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypotension, reflex tachycardia, headache, IV-site phlebitis.
drugs.contraindications: Severe aortic stenosis, advanced AV block.

review.notes: Useful for sustained BP control where esmolol is unsuitable (e.g. asthma).

doses.indication: Hypoxic respiratory failure with PPHN (term / late preterm)
doses.comments: If PaO₂ rises ≥ 60 torr within 4 h, decrease to 5 ppm. Wean FiO₂ first; when FiO₂ < 0.6, wean iNO by 1 ppm every ~4 h. Stop when stable on 1 ppm × 4 h.

drugs.monitoring: Continuous SpO₂, pre/post-ductal SpO₂, ABG, methaemoglobin level (every 12 h initially), NO₂ (gas analyser), echocardiogram.
drugs.adverse_effects: Methaemoglobinaemia, NO₂ toxicity (lung injury), rebound pulmonary hypertension on abrupt withdrawal, bleeding (platelet inhibition — minor).
drugs.contraindications: Right-to-left shunt-dependent congenital heart disease (e.g. severe coarctation, interrupted arch); severe LV dysfunction (relative — may worsen pulmonary oedema).

review.notes: Routine use in preterm < 34 weeks NOT recommended (no benefit, possible harm). Echo to rule out left heart obstruction is mandatory before starting.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_min_range: 0.2–2 mcg/kg/min usual; higher rates may be required
doses.comments: Titrate every 30 min to target BP. PediAid Emergency NICU/PICU calculator handles dilution.

drugs.monitoring: Continuous BP, HR, ECG, urine output, perfusion, IV site (extravasation → necrosis).
drugs.adverse_effects: Tachyarrhythmia, peripheral / mesenteric / renal vasoconstriction, hyperglycaemia, IV infiltration → severe necrosis.
drugs.incompatibilities: Do NOT mix with sodium bicarbonate (inactivates).

review.notes: Treat extravasation with phentolamine 1 mg/mL local infiltration.

doses.comments: Apply half dose to each side of mouth via dropper. Avoid feeding for 5–10 min after.

drugs.monitoring: Resolution of thrush.
drugs.adverse_effects: Negligible at oral / topical doses (poorly absorbed).

review.notes: Some Indian units use oral fluconazole 6 mg/kg/day twice weekly for invasive candidiasis prophylaxis instead of nystatin in extreme preterm.

doses.comments: Tachyphylaxis common after several days.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_min_range: Titrate up by 1 mcg/kg/hr every 24 h ; max 10 mcg/kg/hr
doses.duration: Wean over 2–7 days when chyle drainage falls

drugs.monitoring: Glucose, GH, IGF-1 (chronic), thyroid, gallbladder USG (long-term), GI symptoms.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hyperglycaemia / hypoglycaemia, diarrhoea, bile-stone formation (chronic), necrotising enterocolitis (case reports in preterm), growth suppression (chronic).

review.notes: Preterm — caution due to possible NEC association.

drugs.monitoring: Watch for infection (NEC, pneumonia).
drugs.adverse_effects: Class — increased C. diff, NEC, pneumonia, hypomagnesaemia (chronic).

review.notes: Use shortest course possible.

doses.duration: 5 days (longer in critical illness or immunosuppression)
doses.comments: Initiate within 48 h of symptom onset (exceptions for severe / progressive illness).

drugs.monitoring: Clinical response.
drugs.adverse_effects: Vomiting, diarrhoea, rare neuropsychiatric events (older children).

drugs.monitoring: CBC, LFTs (transaminitis), RFT.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hepatic transaminitis (more than nafcillin), neutropenia, rash, interstitial nephritis.

review.notes: Cefazolin is alternative for MSSA. Vancomycin if MRSA.

doses.indication: RSV prophylaxis (high-risk preterm / CHD / chronic lung disease)
doses.comments: Continue throughout RSV season once started. Discontinue if breakthrough RSV hospitalisation. Consider post-CPB / ECMO redose. NIRSEVIMAB is preferred where available.

drugs.monitoring: Clinical signs of RSV infection.
drugs.adverse_effects: Local injection site reactions, mild fever; rare anaphylaxis.

review.notes: Nirsevimab (single-dose, longer-acting) is replacing palivizumab globally where available.

doses.comments: Test dose 0.02 mg/kg first to assess responsiveness.

drugs.monitoring: Train-of-four, ventilator synchrony, eye and pressure-area care.
drugs.adverse_effects: Tachycardia (vagolytic effect), prolonged paralysis with hepatic / renal impairment, increased BP.
drugs.incompatibilities: Do NOT mix with thiopental.

review.notes: Long duration — useful for sustained paralysis. Vecuronium / rocuronium have less hepatic accumulation.

doses.indication: Severe GERD / stress ulcer prophylaxis (refractory to H₂RA)
doses.comments: From PK studies in neonates — 2.5 mg orally daily provides similar exposure to 40 mg in adults.

drugs.monitoring: Watch for infection (NEC, pneumonia), Mg, B12 (chronic).
drugs.adverse_effects: Increased risk of Clostridioides difficile, NEC, pneumonia (with prolonged use). Hypomagnesaemia. Cobalamin / Fe deficiency (chronic).

review.notes: Long-term PPI use in preterm has been associated with NEC and late-onset sepsis — use shortest course possible.

drugs.category: Smooth-muscle relaxant — used to maintain arterial line patency

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 30 mg per 250 mL (≈ 0.12 mg/mL) of arterial line infusion
doses.comments: Add to standard heparinised saline arterial flush.

drugs.monitoring: Line patency, BP.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypotension (rare from infusion line concentration), arrhythmia.

review.notes: Many neonatal units no longer use papaverine — heparinised saline alone is sufficient.

india_formulations.strength: 10 mg/mL — 1000 mg in 100 mL glass vial
india_formulations.notes: Single-use vial. Use within 6 h of opening. Always check mg vs mL — black-box error source.
Confirm strength on the bottle — 100 vs 150 mg/mL exists.

india_formulations.strength: 125 mg / 5 mL (= 25 mg/mL); 250 mg / 5 mL (= 50 mg/mL)
india_formulations.notes: Most common paediatric strength is 125 mg / 5 mL.
Variable absorption — IV preferred when accurate dose needed.

doses.comments: Withdraw exact dose into syringe; volume in a neonate should always be ≤ 7.5 mL.
May be considered when NSAIDs (indomethacin/ibuprofen) are contraindicated or have failed. A second course may be required.

drugs.monitoring: Pain score; temperature; LFTs (especially if > 48 h of therapy or hepatic risk).
drugs.adverse_effects: IV — injection-site pain (15%), vomiting (5%). Modest reduction in BP and HR may occur in neonates after IV administration. Hepatotoxicity at supra-therapeutic doses or prolonged therapy. Rare serious skin reactions (SJS / TEN / AGEP). Epidemiological association (not causation) with later asthma, eczema, atopy.
drugs.contraindications: Severe hepatic impairment / severe active liver disease; hypersensitivity. Caution in G6PD deficiency, severe renal impairment, dehydration.
drugs.renal_adjustment: Use with caution in severe renal impairment.
drugs.hepatic_adjustment: Avoid IV formulation in severe hepatic impairment.
drugs.incompatibilities: Avoid mixing with other IV medications in the same syringe/line.
drugs.reconstitution: Ready-to-use IV vial 1000 mg / 100 mL. Withdraw dose into syringe and administer within 6 h.
drugs.black_box_warning: Confirm dose in mg (not mL); patients < 50 kg need weight-based dose; do not exceed daily max from all routes/sources. Acute hepatic failure and death have occurred with overdose.

cross_checks.source: WHO Model Formulary for Children 2024 — paracetamol
cross_checks.agreement: agrees on 10–15 mg/kg PO/PR every 6–8 h, max 60 mg/kg/day under 1 month
agrees on IV 12.5 mg/kg q6h ≥ 32 wk PMA, max 50 mg/kg/day

doses.frequency: every 12 h × first 7 days, then every 8 hours

drugs.monitoring: Clinical response; CBC, electrolytes (large volumes of K-penicillin → hyperkalaemia).
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypersensitivity (rare in neonates), seizures at very high CSF doses, eosinophilia, rare interstitial nephritis.
drugs.incompatibilities: Aminoglycosides — separate line.

review.notes: USE ONLY aqueous crystalline penicillin G (potassium or sodium) for IV — NOT benzathine or procaine. 1 mg = 1600 IU.

doses.comments: Use ONLY for asymptomatic, well-screened low-risk infants. Symptomatic / proven congenital syphilis needs aqueous penicillin G IV × 10 days.

drugs.monitoring: VDRL / RPR titre at 3, 6, 12 months; CSF analysis if indicated.
drugs.adverse_effects: Pain at injection site, hypersensitivity reactions, severe pain / cyanosis if injected near nerve / IV (NEVER GIVE IV).
drugs.black_box_warning: Inadvertent IV administration has caused cardiopulmonary arrest and death — IM ONLY.

review.notes: Aqueous crystalline penicillin G IV × 10 days is the preferred regimen for proven / probable congenital syphilis.

doses.indication: Congenital syphilis (when aqueous penicillin G unavailable)

drugs.monitoring: VDRL / RPR titre at 3, 6, 12 months; LFTs.
drugs.adverse_effects: Pain at injection site, hypersensitivity, procaine-related reactions (anxiety, delirium — rare).

review.notes: Procaine penicillin is alternative when aqueous penicillin G IV is unavailable.

doses.frequency: Repeat as needed; or by infusion in burst-suppression protocol

drugs.monitoring: RR, SpO₂, BP, EEG (burst suppression in status), level (target 20–40 mcg/mL for status).
drugs.adverse_effects: Respiratory depression, hypotension, prolonged sedation, immunosuppression.
drugs.contraindications: Severe respiratory depression without ventilation; porphyria.

review.notes: Reserve for refractory status epilepticus. Phenobarbital is first-line.

doses.comments: If seizures persist 15 min after first load, give additional 10–20 mg/kg up to total 40 mg/kg.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 1.5 mg/kg/dose every 8 h OR 2.25 mg/kg/dose every 12 h

doses.frequency: Started 12 h after the loading dose

doses.comments: Wean by 20% every other day based on Finnegan score.

drugs.monitoring: Continuous SpO₂, RR (respiratory depression), level of consciousness, drug level (target 20–40 mcg/mL for status epilepticus), LFTs.
drugs.adverse_effects: Respiratory depression, sedation, hypotension, immunosuppression, paradoxical hyperactivity (later).
drugs.contraindications: Severe respiratory depression without ventilation; porphyria.
drugs.black_box_warning: Concomitant use with opioids → profound sedation, respiratory depression, coma, death.

review.notes: First-line for neonatal seizures across most Indian and international guidelines.

doses.route: Subcutaneous (local infiltration around the affected area)
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Inject 0.5 mg/mL solution into affected area; 1–5 mL total depending on size of infiltrate

drugs.monitoring: BP (hypotension after systemic absorption), perfusion of affected limb, infiltrate resolution.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypotension, tachycardia, GI upset (if systemic absorption), nasal congestion.

review.notes: Crucial NICU bedside drug — must be available wherever catecholamines are infused. Dilute 5 mg vial in 9 mL NS = 0.5 mg/mL.

doses.comments: Increases SVR and reduces R-to-L shunting across VSD.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_min_range: 2–5 mcg/kg/min, titrate to oxygenation while avoiding hypotension

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 1 drop in eye ≥ 10 min before exam
doses.comments: Use only 2.5 % (NEVER 10 %). Apply pressure to lacrimal sac during and 2 min after to minimise systemic absorption.

drugs.monitoring: BP, HR.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypertension, reflex bradycardia, decreased renal / GI perfusion at high dose. Systemic effects of ophthalmic 10 % (HTN, hypothermia).

doses.infusion_rate: Do not exceed 1–3 mg/kg/min (or 50 mg/min, whichever is slower)
doses.comments: Watch for severe hypotension and arrhythmia.

drugs.monitoring: Continuous ECG (bradycardia, AV block) and BP during IV infusion. Level (target 10–20 mcg/mL total / 1–2 mcg/mL free). LFTs.
drugs.adverse_effects: Cardiac arrhythmia / bradycardia / hypotension during infusion (purple-glove syndrome with extravasation), gingival hyperplasia, hepatotoxicity, blood dyscrasias, hypersensitivity (DRESS, SJS).
drugs.contraindications: HLA-B*15:02 carrier (SJS risk); severe hepatic impairment; hypersensitivity. Concomitant delavirdine.
drugs.black_box_warning: IV infusion rate ≤ 1–3 mg/kg/min — severe hypotension and arrhythmia at higher rates. Continuous cardiac monitoring required.
drugs.incompatibilities: Will precipitate in dextrose-containing solutions. Use NS only. Flush IV line before and after.

review.notes: Fosphenytoin (phenytoin pro-drug, dosed as PE) is gentler on tissue but expensive — Neofax page 431.

drugs.monitoring: CBC, RFT, electrolytes (Na load).
drugs.adverse_effects: Same as pip-tazo; high sodium load (1.85 mEq Na per gram).

review.notes: Plain piperacillin is rarely available now; pip-tazo is the standard formulation.

drugs.monitoring: Clinical response; CBC, RFT, electrolytes, LFTs.
drugs.adverse_effects: Diarrhoea, eosinophilia, transaminitis, neutropenia (especially > 7 days), thrombocytosis, AKI (especially with concurrent vancomycin).

doses.frequency: Single initial dose; up to 2 subsequent doses of 1.25 mL/kg (100 mg/kg) every 12 h if persistent need
doses.comments: For prophylaxis: within 10–30 min of birth. For rescue: within 2 h of birth. May redose more frequently than every 12 h if surfactant inactivated by infection / meconium / blood.

drugs.monitoring: Continuous SpO₂, HR, chest expansion, FiO₂ requirement.
drugs.adverse_effects: Transient bradycardia, desaturation, ETT block, pulmonary haemorrhage (rare), air leak.

review.notes: Poractant alfa (200 mg/kg first dose) has lower mortality vs beractant (100 mg/kg) per Cochrane meta-analysis. Most-used surfactant in India.

doses.route: IV (over 1 h, peripheral max 40 mmol/L; central max 80 mmol/L)

drugs.administration: 1 g KCl = 13.4 mEq K⁺. 1 mEq K⁺ = 74.6 mg KCl. Always document mEq, not mg of salt.
drugs.monitoring: ECG (peaked T, QT shortening, arrhythmia), serum K⁺ every 4–6 h during correction.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hyperkalaemia (cardiac arrest), phlebitis, IV-site pain.
drugs.incompatibilities: Do NOT push undiluted IV — fatal cardiac arrest.

review.notes: PediAid has a Potassium Correction calculator.

doses.indication: Radiation emergency thyroid protection (under public-health direction)
doses.frequency: once daily until exposure risk passes

drugs.monitoring: TFTs.
drugs.adverse_effects: Goitre, hypothyroidism (chronic), GI upset, rash, salivary gland inflammation.

review.notes: Public-health emergency only; not for routine NICU use.

doses.comments: Premature neonates — use lowest dose. Continuous ECG monitoring mandatory.

drugs.monitoring: Continuous ECG (QRS / QT), BP, serum procainamide + N-acetylprocainamide (NAPA) levels, ANA (lupus-like reaction with chronic use).
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypotension during infusion, QT prolongation, torsades, lupus-like reaction (chronic), agranulocytosis, AV block.
drugs.contraindications: AV block, severe heart failure, lupus.

review.notes: Largely replaced by amiodarone in PALS 2020 for paediatric refractory arrhythmia.

doses.indication: Infantile haemangioma (≥ 5 weeks of age, ≥ 2 kg)
doses.loading_dose_per_kg: Week 1: 0.6 mg/kg/dose BD ; Week 2: 1.1 mg/kg/dose BD ; Week 3 onwards: 1.7 mg/kg/dose BD
doses.frequency: twice daily, ≥ 9 hours apart, with feeds
doses.duration: 6 months (typical); continue until full involution / 1 yr of age

drugs.monitoring: HR, BP, glucose (hypoglycaemia in neonates), bronchospasm signs.
drugs.adverse_effects: Bradycardia, hypotension, hypoglycaemia (especially in fasting / illness), bronchospasm, sleep disturbance.
drugs.contraindications: Bradyarrhythmia / heart block (without pacemaker), severe asthma, cardiogenic shock, congenital heart block.

review.notes: Always feed with each propranolol dose to avoid hypoglycaemia. Hold on intercurrent illness with poor feeding.

doses.indication: Reverse heparin (UFH) — by time elapsed since last heparin
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: < 30 min since heparin: 1 mg per 100 U heparin given ; 30–60 min: 0.5–0.75 mg per 100 U ; 60–120 min: 0.375–0.5 mg per 100 U ; > 120 min: 0.25–0.375 mg per 100 U
Dalteparin: 1 mg per 100 anti-Xa IU. Enoxaparin: 1 mg per 1 mg of enoxaparin given < 8 h ago (0.5 mg per 1 mg if 8–12 h ago)

drugs.monitoring: Continuous BP, HR; aPTT 5 min and 2–4 h after; ACT (ECMO).
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypotension, bradycardia, anaphylaxis (especially in fish allergy / prior protamine exposure / NPH-insulin patients), pulmonary hypertension.

review.notes: Avoid rapid push — risk of histamine-mediated hypotension.

doses.indication: Severe congenital protein C deficiency / purpura fulminans — acute episode

doses.comments: Adjust to peak protein C activity 100 %, trough > 25 %.

drugs.monitoring: Protein C activity, bleeding signs, hypersensitivity.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypersensitivity, transient bleeding (rebound), thromboembolism.

review.notes: Initiate only with paediatric haematology team.

doses.frequency: Single dose with EEG monitoring; if seizures stop within minutes, diagnostic

doses.comments: Higher doses may be required during intercurrent illness.

drugs.monitoring: Seizure response, neurodevelopmental progress.
drugs.adverse_effects: High-dose IV pyridoxine: rare apnoea, hypotonia. Chronic high-dose: peripheral neuropathy.

review.notes: Always trial in any neonatal refractory seizure NOT controlled by phenobarbital + phenytoin.

doses.comments: Central catheter administration recommended due to infusion-site phlebitis.

drugs.monitoring: LFTs, CBC, infusion-site reaction.
drugs.adverse_effects: Infusion-site phlebitis, transaminitis, arthralgia / myalgia, hyperbilirubinaemia.

review.notes: Reserve for confirmed VRE / MDR. Linezolid / daptomycin are usually preferred.

doses.comments: Lower 0.12 mg / 0.02 mL effective in pilot studies. Premedicate with topical anaesthetic (proparacaine / tetracaine) and ophthalmic antiseptic (povidone iodine 5 % or 10 %). Topical antibiotic drops × 7 days post-procedure.

drugs.monitoring: Serial retinal exam; systemic monitoring (less systemic exposure than bevacizumab).
drugs.adverse_effects: Endophthalmitis (rare), retinal detachment, transient IOP rise.

review.notes: Ranibizumab has lower systemic VEGF suppression than bevacizumab — preferred where availability and cost permit.

doses.indication: Historical reference only — DO NOT USE
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Historical — PO 2 mg/kg q8h ; IV term 1.5 mg/kg q8h ; IV preterm 0.5 mg/kg q12h ; continuous infusion 0.0625 mg/kg/hr
doses.comments: WITHDRAWN GLOBALLY — N-Nitrosodimethylamine (NDMA) impurity. Use famotidine / pantoprazole instead.

drugs.monitoring: N/A.
drugs.adverse_effects: Withdrawn for NDMA carcinogenicity concern.

review.notes: Included for completeness only — DO NOT USE. Substitute with famotidine 0.25–1 mg/kg/day.

doses.indication: Adjunct for staph — endocarditis / prosthetic infection (with prosthetic material)
Haemophilus influenzae type b prophylaxis (close contacts)

drugs.monitoring: LFTs, CBC, drug interactions (potent CYP3A4 inducer — lowers levels of many drugs).
drugs.adverse_effects: Orange-red discolouration of secretions / urine (harmless), transaminitis, thrombocytopenia, rash.

cross_checks.source: AAP Red Book — Hib, meningococcal prophylaxis

review.notes: TB-specific dosing (10–20 mg/kg/day) is per IAP/RNTCP guidelines and not detailed here.

doses.comments: Onset ~75 sec at 0.6 mg/kg.

drugs.monitoring: Train-of-four, ventilator synchrony, eye and pressure-area care.
drugs.adverse_effects: Tachycardia (mild), prolonged paralysis with hepatic / renal impairment, anaphylaxis (rare but documented).
drugs.contraindications: Use only by trained clinicians familiar with airway management.

review.notes: Reverse with sugammadex 16 mg/kg (immediate) or 4 mg/kg (deep block). Vecuronium is alternative for prolonged paralysis.

doses.comments: Confirm retinal vascularisation in extreme preterm before starting.
Highly variable PK — careful titration with O₂ and BP monitoring.

drugs.monitoring: BP (systemic hypotension), oxygenation, retinal exam (extremely preterm — not before retinal vascularisation).
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypotension, retinal effects, gastrointestinal, priapism (rare in newborn).
drugs.contraindications: Concurrent nitrates, severe hypotension, retinal vascular immaturity (extreme preterm).

review.notes: Used as bridge / adjunct to iNO. STRIDER trial showed no benefit and possible harm for PPROM-associated pulmonary hypoplasia.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 20 mg per dose (0.5 mL of 40 mg/mL)
doses.frequency: as needed, after meals and at bedtime

drugs.monitoring: Symptom relief.
drugs.adverse_effects: None significant — non-absorbed.

review.notes: Evidence for efficacy in infantile colic is limited.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: HCO₃ needed (mEq) = base deficit (mEq/L) × 0.3 × wt (kg) — give half, reassess

doses.indication: Resuscitation (routine use NOT recommended in NRP)
doses.comments: Establish ventilation FIRST. Routine bicarbonate in cardiac arrest is not recommended.

drugs.administration: Use 4.2 % (0.5 mEq/mL) for neonates — NEVER 8.4 % undiluted. Slow infusion to avoid rapid pH shifts → IVH.
drugs.monitoring: Blood gas, electrolytes (Na, K), BP.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypernatraemia, hyperosmolarity, IVH (preterm — rapid push), tissue necrosis on extravasation, paradoxical CSF acidosis.
drugs.contraindications: Hypoventilation (respiratory acidosis without ventilation support), metabolic alkalosis, hypocalcaemia.
drugs.incompatibilities: Do NOT mix with calcium, catecholamines (adrenaline / noradrenaline / dopamine — inactivated).

review.notes: Always document salt strength: 4.2 % = 0.5 mEq/mL, 8.4 % = 1 mEq/mL.

doses.comments: Lactated Ringer's is acceptable alternative. Reassess for hepatomegaly before further boluses.

drugs.monitoring: Serum Na, urine output, weight, fluid balance.
drugs.adverse_effects: Volume overload, hypernatraemia, hyperchloraemic acidosis (large volumes).

review.notes: Hypertonic saline (3 %) — for symptomatic hyponatraemia, dose 3–5 mL/kg over 15–30 min (raises serum Na ~ 3 mEq/L).

doses.indication: Phosphate supplementation in PN (less precipitation than KPhos / NaPhos)
doses.comments: Less risk of Ca-P precipitation in PN bag than inorganic phosphate.

drugs.monitoring: Serum P, Ca, Mg.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hyperphosphataemia, hypocalcaemia, hypernatraemia.

review.notes: Preferred organic phosphate for paediatric PN — lower precipitation risk than KPhos.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_min_range: Titrate to BP, MAX 10 mcg/kg/min — but no longer than 10 min at max rate
doses.comments: Risk of cyanide / thiocyanate toxicity beyond 72 h or rates > 2 mcg/kg/min — switch to alternative if prolonged.

drugs.administration: Protect from light (light wrap on bag and tubing). Use central vein. Mix with D5W only.
drugs.monitoring: Continuous arterial BP, ECG; serum thiocyanate level (> 50 mg/L toxic) if used > 48 h or in renal impairment; serum cyanide / lactate (if metabolic acidosis develops).
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypotension (rapid onset), cyanide toxicity, thiocyanate accumulation (renal impairment), methaemoglobinaemia.
drugs.contraindications: Compensatory hypertension (e.g. coarctation, AV shunt); severe hepatic impairment; high-output heart failure.
drugs.renal_adjustment: If GFR < 30 mL/min/1.73 m²: limit mean rate < 3 mcg/kg/min. Anuria: limit < 1 mcg/kg/min.

review.notes: Co-administer hydroxocobalamin or sodium thiosulfate for cyanide protection if prolonged use.

drugs.category: Urea cycle disorder — alternative pathway nitrogen excretion

doses.indication: Acute hyperammonaemia (urea cycle disorders, pending diagnosis)
doses.loading_dose_per_kg: Sodium phenylacetate 250 mg/kg + Sodium benzoate 250 mg/kg + Arginine HCl 600 mg/kg over 90–120 min
doses.dose_per_kg_per_min_range: Maintenance: each at 250 mg/kg/day (range 250–500 mg/kg/day) + Arginine HCl 250 mg/kg/day continuous infusion
doses.comments: Premedicate with ondansetron 0.15 mg/kg IV (vomiting). Co-administer dextrose for caloric load. Initiate dialysis preparation for ammonia > 500 mcmol/L.

drugs.monitoring: Plasma ammonia (every 2–4 h), electrolytes, glucose, LFT, plasma amino acids, BP.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hyperventilation (alkalosis), vomiting, hypokalaemia, hypotension on rapid loading. Severe acidosis at overdose.

review.notes: Initiate only with metabolic medicine team. Prepare for haemodialysis simultaneously if ammonia > 500 mcmol/L or rising despite therapy.

doses.frequency: every 12 hours, increased gradually under monitoring
doses.comments: INITIATE in hospital with continuous ECG × 3 days. Baseline QTc must be < 450 ms.

drugs.monitoring: Continuous ECG (QTc, AV block), K, Mg, RFT.
drugs.adverse_effects: Torsades de pointes (especially with hypoK / hypoMg), bradycardia, AV block, hypotension, bronchospasm.
drugs.contraindications: Baseline QTc > 450 ms, severe sinus bradycardia, AV block (without pacemaker), bronchial asthma, decompensated heart failure.
drugs.renal_adjustment: Required — calculate CrCl to set interval.

review.notes: Initiate only in hospital with cardiologist input.

doses.indication: Diuresis (BPD, CHF, ascites) — often combined with HCTZ

drugs.monitoring: Electrolytes (K — risk of hyperkalaemia), Na, urine output, BP, RFT.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hyperkalaemia, gynaecomastia (chronic), GI upset.
drugs.contraindications: Anuria, hyperkalaemia, severe renal impairment, Addison disease.

review.notes: Often paired with thiazide (HCTZ) for BPD chronic diuresis — counterbalances K loss.

doses.indication: Tracheal intubation (rare in NICU — rocuronium preferred)
doses.frequency: Single immediately before intubation; max cumulative 4 mg/kg
doses.comments: Onset ~ 30–60 sec, duration ~ 5 min.

drugs.monitoring: ECG, SpO₂, airway, K (especially in burns / muscular dystrophy / prolonged immobilisation — hyperkalaemia risk).
drugs.adverse_effects: Hyperkalaemia (especially burns, crush injury, muscular disease — risk of cardiac arrest), bradycardia (especially repeat doses), masseter spasm, malignant hyperthermia.
drugs.contraindications: Burn / crush injury > 24 h, paraplegia / muscular dystrophy, hyperkalaemia, malignant hyperthermia history. Many NICU experts AVOID due to bradycardia and HyperK risk in sick neonates.
drugs.black_box_warning: Risk of cardiac arrest from acute hyperkalaemia, especially in undiagnosed muscle / metabolic disease — rocuronium preferred for paediatric intubation.

review.notes: Rocuronium is preferred over succinylcholine for routine NICU intubation.

doses.indication: Procedural pain (heel prick, IV insertion, NG insertion, injections)
doses.frequency: 2 minutes before painful procedure ; offer pacifier after
doses.comments: Effect peaks at 2 min, lasts ~ 5 min.

drugs.monitoring: Pain score (NIPS, PIPP, FLACC).
drugs.adverse_effects: Negligible. No glucose effect at small volumes.
drugs.contraindications: NPO status (relative — avoid in suspected NEC, recent surgery).

cross_checks.source: Cochrane review — Sucrose for procedural pain

review.notes: Combine with non-nutritive sucking (pacifier) and skin-to-skin / facilitated tucking for maximal analgesia.

doses.indication: Severe metabolic acidosis (especially when Na load must be limited)
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 1–2 mmol/kg per dose (= 3.3–6.6 mL/kg of 0.3 M solution)
doses.comments: Dose (mL) = wt(kg) × base deficit (mEq/L) for 0.3 M solution. Short-term use only.

drugs.monitoring: Blood gas, electrolytes (K — risk of hyperkalaemia), glucose (hypoglycaemia), RFT.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hyperkalaemia, hypoglycaemia, respiratory depression (apnoea), tissue necrosis on extravasation.

review.notes: Useful when sodium load (NaHCO₃) must be limited (e.g. heart failure, hypernatraemia). Limited recent data.

doses.indication: Gram-negative sepsis (limited use — withdrawn in US)

drugs.monitoring: CBC, RFT, electrolytes (high Na).
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypokalaemia, sodium overload (5.2 mEq Na per gram), bleeding (platelet inhibition), rash.

review.notes: Timentin discontinued in US since 2015. Use piperacillin-tazobactam instead.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: PMA-based: ≤ 29 wk: 5 mg/kg q48h (0–7 d), 4 mg/kg q36h (8–28 d), q24h (≥ 29 d) ; 30–34 wk: 4.5 mg/kg q36h (0–7 d), 4 mg/kg q24h (≥ 8 d) ; ≥ 35 wk: 4 mg/kg q24h

drugs.monitoring: Therapeutic levels — Peak (30 min post): 5–12 mg/L (target 8–13 for extended-interval) ; Trough: < 1 mg/L. Audiology screen.
drugs.adverse_effects: Same as gentamicin — nephrotoxic, oto- and vestibular toxic.
drugs.black_box_warning: Aminoglycoside class — neurotoxicity, ototoxicity, nephrotoxicity. Risk increased with renal impairment, dehydration, prolonged therapy.

review.notes: Tobramycin is preferred over gentamicin against Pseudomonas (slightly lower MICs).

doses.indication: Adjunct to therapeutic hypothermia for HIE (investigational)
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Investigational — pilot studies used 5–10 mg/kg/dose loading then 5 mg/kg/day maintenance
doses.comments: Pilot RCT (n=44) did NOT show reduction in mortality / disability. NOT routine clinical use.

drugs.monitoring: Serum bicarbonate (metabolic acidosis), level (target 5–20 mcg/mL where measured), feeding, weight.
drugs.adverse_effects: Metabolic acidosis (carbonic anhydrase inhibition), hyperthermia (oligohidrosis), nephrolithiasis, sedation, weight loss / poor feeding.

review.notes: Not recommended for routine HIE neuroprotection — only investigational.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 1 drop in eye ≥ 10 min before procedure
doses.comments: Use only 0.5 % concentration in neonates. Apply pressure to lacrimal sac during + 2 min after to minimise systemic absorption. Often combined with phenylephrine 2.5 %.

drugs.monitoring: HR, BP, GI distension (anticholinergic systemic effects).
drugs.adverse_effects: Tachycardia, abdominal distension, transient hyperglycaemia.

review.notes: Most NICUs use a combined drop (e.g. cyclopentolate 0.2 % + phenylephrine 1 % = Cyclomydril) — single-drop convenience.

drugs.monitoring: LFTs (especially conjugated bilirubin), GGT.
drugs.adverse_effects: Diarrhoea (most common), abdominal pain, rash.
drugs.contraindications: Acute cholangitis, complete biliary obstruction, hepatic failure.

review.notes: Common adjunct in PN-dependent infants with cholestasis.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: Hold if ANC < 500 (recheck) ; resume at 50 % when ANC > 750 ; discontinue if ANC drops < 500 again

drugs.monitoring: CBC weekly × 6 weeks then monthly (neutropenia in ~ 20 %), LFTs, RFT, audiology, viral load.
drugs.adverse_effects: Neutropenia (most common), thrombocytopenia, hepatotoxicity, mutagenic / carcinogenic potential.
drugs.contraindications: ANC < 500 (without dose reduction), platelets < 25k, severe renal impairment.
drugs.black_box_warning: Bone marrow suppression, mutagenic / carcinogenic potential.

review.notes: Oral valganciclovir × 6 months is the preferred course for symptomatic congenital CMV (improves audiologic and neurodevelopmental outcomes per Kimberlin 2015 NEJM).

doses.indication: MRSA / coagulase-neg staph / penicillin-resistant strep sepsis

doses.frequency: every 8–48 hours by PMA, weight, and creatinine

drugs.monitoring: Therapeutic levels — Trough (just pre-next-dose) 10–20 mg/L (some target AUC₂₄/MIC ≥ 400). Renal function (Cr, urine output) every 2–3 days. Audiology screen if prolonged or with concurrent ototoxic drugs.
drugs.adverse_effects: Nephrotoxicity (especially with concurrent piperacillin-tazobactam, aminoglycosides, contrast), 'red-man syndrome' (rapid infusion — flushing, hypotension), ototoxicity (rare), thrombocytopenia, neutropenia.
drugs.renal_adjustment: Extend interval; redose by trough levels.
drugs.incompatibilities: Many — use dedicated line where possible.

review.notes: AUC-guided dosing is replacing trough-only dosing in newer guidelines.

doses.indication: Prevention / attenuation of varicella in exposed neonate (mother developed varicella 5 days before to 2 days after delivery, or other high-risk contact)
doses.frequency: Single dose, ideally within 96 h of exposure (up to 10 days)
doses.comments: Repeat full dose if a new exposure occurs > 3 weeks after.

drugs.monitoring: Watch for varicella signs for 28 days post-exposure (incubation may be prolonged after VZIG).
drugs.adverse_effects: Local injection-site discomfort; rare hypersensitivity.

review.notes: Limited availability in India — IVIG can be used as alternative if VZIG unavailable.

doses.frequency: every 1–2 hours as needed by clinical paralysis
doses.comments: May give as continuous infusion 0.05–0.15 mg/kg/hr.

drugs.monitoring: Train-of-four (where available), ventilator synchrony, eye protection, pressure-area care.
drugs.adverse_effects: Tachycardia (mild), prolonged paralysis with renal/hepatic impairment, hypotension (rare).
drugs.contraindications: Use only by trained clinicians familiar with airway management.
drugs.black_box_warning: Should be administered only by adequately trained individuals familiar with its actions and hazards.
drugs.incompatibilities: Do NOT mix with thiopental.

review.notes: Reverse with sugammadex (where available) or neostigmine + atropine.

india_formulations.strength: 60,000 IU per sachet (high dose for deficiency)

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 200–400 IU/day; increase to 400 IU/day when BW ~ 1500 g and on full feeds

drugs.monitoring: Serum 25-OH-vitamin D (target 30–80 ng/mL), Ca, P, alk-phos.
drugs.adverse_effects: Hypercalcaemia, hypercalciuria, hyperphosphataemia (chronic over-dose), nephrocalcinosis.

review.notes: All exclusively breastfed neonates need vitamin D supplementation from birth.

doses.comments: 1 mg α-tocopherol = 1.49 IU natural (d-form) = 2.22 IU synthetic (dl-form). Do NOT co-administer with iron — reduces iron absorption.

drugs.monitoring: Routine clinical; serum vitamin E if deficiency suspected.
drugs.adverse_effects: Generally well tolerated; high IV doses linked to hepatic / pulmonary / NEC events historically.
drugs.contraindications: Vitamin K antagonism risk at high doses.

doses.indication: VKDB prophylaxis — oral alternative when IM refused
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 2 mg PO at birth, then 1 mg/week × 3 months  OR  2 mg PO at birth, 4–6 days, and 4–6 weeks
doses.comments: AVOID oral route in preterm, ill, malabsorption, cholestasis, or maternal anticonvulsants.

doses.route: SC (preferred) or slow IV (over ≥ 1 minute, not exceeding 1 mg/min)
doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 1 mg/dose; higher doses (1–10 mg) have been used IV
doses.comments: Avoid IM in known coagulopathy. Anaphylaxis risk with IV — dilute and infuse slowly.

drugs.monitoring: Coagulation (PT/INR) in treatment of VKDB or therapeutic use.
drugs.adverse_effects: Anaphylaxis with IV / IM (rare but reported), pain at injection site, transient hyperbilirubinaemia.
drugs.black_box_warning: Severe hypersensitivity reactions including anaphylaxis with IV / IM use — dilute and infuse slowly. Avoid rapid IV.

review.notes: IM is the preferred route in India and globally; oral schedule is for parental refusal of IM.

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 4 mg/kg/dose PO BD (or 3 mg/kg/dose IV BD if no oral)
doses.duration: 4–6 weeks (per protocol; combine with NVP per India NACO)

doses.dose_per_kg_per_dose: 2 to < 3 kg: 10 mg q12h ; 3 to < 4 kg: 15 mg q12h ; 4 to < 5 kg: 20 mg q12h
Birth–2 wk: 2 mg/kg q12h PO (or 1.5 mg/kg q12h IV) ; 2 wk–4–6 wk: 3 mg/kg q12h PO (or 2.25 mg/kg q12h IV)

drugs.monitoring: CBC (anaemia, neutropenia), LFTs, lactate (lactic acidosis), HIV PCR (viral load).
drugs.adverse_effects: Anaemia, neutropenia, transaminitis, lactic acidosis (rare in neonates — risk over months).
drugs.contraindications: Severe anaemia / neutropenia (relative — may need dose adjustment).
drugs.black_box_warning: Bone marrow suppression (anaemia, neutropenia); lactic acidosis with hepatic steatosis (rare); myopathy (chronic use).

review.notes: Standard India PMTCT regimen: AZT + NVP from birth × 6 weeks for high-risk infants.
